faq.question: What is Australia visa fee for Indians in 2026?
faq.answer: Australia Visitor Visa (Subclass 600) fee is AUD $190. This applies to all streams - Tourist, Business, and Sponsored Family. Fee is paid online via credit/debit card.

faq.question: Is Australia visa fee refundable if rejected?
faq.answer: No, the AUD $190 visa application charge is non-refundable regardless of the outcome. If visa is refused, you will not receive a refund.

faq.question: Are there any additional charges for Australia visa?
faq.answer: Base fee is AUD $190. Additional charges may apply for: biometrics (if requested), health examination (if required), character certificates. Most tourist applicants only pay base fee.

faq.question: Can I pay Australia visa fee in INR?
faq.answer: Visa fee is charged in AUD. You can pay with Indian credit/debit card - bank converts at current exchange rate. Keep international transactions enabled on your card.

faq.question: How long does Australia visa take for Indians?
faq.answer: Processing takes 15-30 days typically. Department statistics: 75% processed within 24 days, 90% within 52 days. Business stream may be faster. Apply 6-8 weeks before travel.

faq.question: Can I get Australia visa urgently?
faq.answer: There is no official expedited processing for Subclass 600. Processing depends on completeness of application and case officer workload. Apply early to avoid delays.

faq.question: Why is my Australia visa taking so long?
faq.answer: Delays may occur due to: peak season, incomplete documentation, additional verification required, health/character checks, or high volume. Check ImmiAccount for any document requests.

faq.question: What documents are required for Australia tourist visa?
faq.answer: Required: Passport (6 months validity), passport photos, bank statements (6 months), employment letter, ITR (3 years), travel itinerary. Recommended: Property documents, invitation letter if visiting family.

faq.question: How much bank balance for Australia visa?
faq.answer: No official minimum, but AUD $5,000+ per person recommended. Show 6 months bank statements with consistent balance. Large last-minute deposits are viewed negatively.

faq.question: Do I need flight tickets for Australia visa?
faq.answer: Confirmed flight tickets are NOT required for application. You can apply without bookings. Only book flights after visa is granted. Show intended travel dates in application.

faq.question: Is travel insurance mandatory for Australia visa?
faq.answer: Travel insurance is NOT mandatory for Subclass 600, but highly recommended. Australia healthcare is expensive. Get coverage for at least AUD $50,000 including medical evacuation.

faq.question: How long can I stay in Australia on tourist visa?
faq.answer: Usually 3 months per visit. Some visas may allow 6 or 12 months. Check your visa grant letter for specific conditions. Cannot exceed total validity period.

faq.question: How long is Australia tourist visa valid?
faq.answer: Typically 12 months from grant date. Multiple entries allowed within validity. Frequent Traveller stream may receive up to 10 years validity.

faq.question: Can I work in Australia on tourist visa?
faq.answer: No, working is NOT permitted on Subclass 600 Visitor Visa. Only tourism and short business activities (meetings, negotiations) allowed. Working illegally leads to visa cancellation and ban.

faq.question: What is Genuine Temporary Entrant (GTE)?
faq.answer: GTE means you genuinely intend to stay temporarily in Australia and return to India. You must demonstrate: ties to India (job, family, property), clear travel purpose, and return plans.

faq.question: How to apply Australia visa from India?
faq.answer: Apply online: 1) Create ImmiAccount at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au, 2) Fill Form 1419, 3) Upload documents, 4) Pay AUD $190, 5) Submit and track. No embassy visit required.

faq.question: Is interview required for Australia visa?
faq.answer: No, interview is NOT required for most Subclass 600 applications. Decision is made based on documents and application. In rare cases, additional information may be requested.

faq.question: Can I visit both Australia and New Zealand on one visa?
faq.answer: No, separate visas required. Australia Subclass 600 is only for Australia. New Zealand requires separate NZeTA or visitor visa. Plan and apply for both if visiting both countries.

faq.question: What if Australia visa is rejected?
faq.answer: You can reapply immediately, but should address rejection reasons. Strengthen weak areas (finances, ties, documents). Visa refusal is recorded and must be declared on future applications.

faq.question: Best time to visit Australia?
faq.answer: Sept-Nov (Spring) and Mar-May (Autumn) have pleasant weather. Dec-Feb is Australian summer (crowded, expensive). Jun-Aug is winter (good for Northern Australia).

faq.question: Is the Australia visa open for Indians?
faq.answer: Yes, Australia visa is open for Indian passport holders. Visa fees vary by visa type and are payable in INR.

faq.question: How much does it cost to get a visa for Australia from India?
faq.answer: Australia visa fees vary by visa type. For example, visitor visa (subclass 600) costs AUD 190.

faq.question: How to get a free visa for Australia?
faq.answer: Australia does not offer free visas for Indian passport holders. All visa types require paid fees. Australia visa starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa with all-inclusive processing.

faq.question: Is a 2 year gap accepted in Australia?
faq.answer: Yes, a 2-year employment gap is generally acceptable for Australia visas if you can explain it convincingly (education, health, family reasons). Document the gap reason clearly in your application. Supporting documents in English strengthen your application.

faq.answer: The Azerbaijan ASAN e-visa starts at {{price}} for standard processing (3-5 days). An express 3-hour option is available at {{price:express}} through Times of Visa. Both are single-entry, 30-day stay visas.

faq.question: Is Azerbaijan e-visa fee refundable?
faq.answer: No, the ASAN e-visa fee is non-refundable regardless of application outcome. Ensure all information is correct before paying to avoid rejection.

faq.question: Can I pay Azerbaijan e-visa fee in Indian rupees?
faq.answer: Yes, pay in Indian rupees when applying through Times of Visa. Standard e-visa is {{price}} and express processing is {{price:express}}. Payment accepted via credit card, debit card, and UPI.

faq.question: How much does urgent Azerbaijan visa cost?
faq.answer: The urgent ASAN e-visa costs {{price:express}} and is processed within 3 hours. Standard processing at {{price}} takes {{processing_time}}. Choose based on your travel timeline.

faq.question: How much does Azerbaijan trip cost from India?
faq.answer: Azerbaijan is affordable. Budget: ₹3,000-5,000/day. Return flights: ₹18,000-30,000 (via Gulf). Hotels from ₹2,000/night. A 5-day trip costs ₹45,000-75,000 including flights.

faq.question: How long does Azerbaijan e-visa take?
faq.answer: Standard ASAN e-visa takes {{processing_time}} at {{price}}. The urgent option delivers in 3 hours for {{price:express}}. Most standard applications are approved within 3 days.

faq.question: Can I get Azerbaijan visa in 3 hours?
faq.answer: Yes, the ASAN urgent e-visa processes within 3 hours for {{price:express}}. Perfect for last-minute travel plans to Baku. Standard processing at {{price}} takes {{processing_time}}.

faq.question: How to check Azerbaijan e-visa status?
faq.answer: Track your application status through Times of Visa at timesofvisa.com. The approved e-visa is sent directly to your registered email address.

faq.question: When should I apply for Azerbaijan e-visa?
faq.answer: Apply 1-2 weeks before travel for standard processing. The e-visa is valid for 90 days from issue, so applying early is safe. For urgent travel, use the 3-hour option.

faq.question: Is Azerbaijan visa on arrival available for Indians?
faq.answer: Azerbaijan does not offer visa on arrival for Indian citizens. Apply through Times of Visa at timesofvisa.com before travel or use urgent 3-hour processing for last-minute plans.

faq.question: What documents are needed for Azerbaijan e-visa?
faq.answer: Only 2 documents needed: (1) Scan of passport bio page, (2) Recent passport-size photo. No bank statements, employment proof, hotel booking, or flight tickets required for the e-visa application.

faq.question: Do I need bank statement for Azerbaijan visa?
faq.answer: No, bank statements are NOT required for the ASAN e-visa application. This is one of the simplest visa applications requiring only passport and photo.

faq.question: Do I need hotel booking for Azerbaijan e-visa?
faq.answer: No, hotel booking is not required for the ASAN e-visa application. However, having accommodation plans is recommended for a smooth immigration process at arrival.

faq.question: Is travel insurance required for Azerbaijan?
faq.answer: Travel insurance is not mandatory for the ASAN e-visa but is recommended. Azerbaijan has good healthcare facilities but costs can be high for foreigners.

faq.question: What photo specifications for Azerbaijan e-visa?
faq.answer: Photo requirements: front-facing, white or light background, recent (within 6 months), clear face, no glasses, neutral expression. Upload in JPEG format, under 2MB.

faq.question: Do I need return ticket for Azerbaijan e-visa?
faq.answer: Return ticket is not required for the e-visa application itself, but carry a return or onward ticket when traveling as immigration may ask for it upon arrival.

faq.question: How long can I stay in Azerbaijan on e-visa?
faq.answer: The ASAN e-visa allows a maximum stay of 30 days. The e-visa itself is valid for 90 days from issue date - you must enter within this period.

faq.question: Can I extend Azerbaijan e-visa?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply for extension at the State Migration Service in Baku before your 30 days expire. Extension requires additional documentation and fees.

faq.question: What happens if I overstay in Azerbaijan?
faq.answer: Overstaying leads to fines (approximately AZN 300-400), possible detention, and deportation. Overstay may result in entry ban for future visits. Always exit or extend on time.

faq.question: Can I work in Azerbaijan on e-visa?
faq.answer: No, the ASAN e-visa is for tourism and short business visits only. Employment requires a work permit and visa sponsored by an Azerbaijani employer.

faq.question: Can I re-enter Azerbaijan on the same e-visa?
faq.answer: The standard ASAN e-visa is single entry. Once you exit, you need a new e-visa to re-enter. Apply for a new one through Times of Visa at timesofvisa.com if you plan to return.

faq.question: Can I apply Azerbaijan e-visa from phone?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply through Times of Visa on your mobile browser at timesofvisa.com. Complete the entire application and payment from your smartphone.

faq.question: Do I need to visit Azerbaijan embassy for visa?
faq.answer: No, the ASAN e-visa is 100% online. No embassy visit needed. Apply through Times of Visa at timesofvisa.com.

faq.question: What if my Azerbaijan e-visa application has errors?
faq.answer: If the e-visa has incorrect details, you cannot edit it after submission. Apply for a new e-visa with correct information. The fee for the incorrect application is not refunded.

faq.question: Is Azerbaijan safe for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: Yes, Azerbaijan is safe for tourists with low crime rates. Baku is modern and well-policed. Avoid the Nagorno-Karabakh region. Locals are friendly and hospitality is part of the culture.

faq.question: Best time to visit Azerbaijan?
faq.answer: April to June and September to October are ideal with pleasant temperatures (15-25°C). Summers (July-August) are hot in Baku. Winters are mild but windy.

faq.question: Are there direct flights from India to Azerbaijan?
faq.answer: No direct flights currently. Best connections via Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Istanbul, or Doha to Baku. Flight time: 7-10 hours with layover. Azerbaijan Airlines (AZAL) operates some seasonal routes.

faq.question: Is vegetarian food available in Azerbaijan?
faq.answer: Azerbaijani cuisine is meat-heavy but vegetarian options exist: kutabs (stuffed flatbread), dolma (grape leaf rolls with rice), salads, and bread/cheese. Indian restaurants available in Baku.

faq.question: What currency is used in Azerbaijan?
faq.answer: Azerbaijani Manat (AZN). 1 AZN ≈ ₹49. Exchange USD in Baku banks or exchange offices. Credit cards accepted in Baku hotels and restaurants. Carry cash for rural areas.

faq.question: Can I travel to Nagorno-Karabakh?
faq.answer: Travel to the Nagorno-Karabakh region is heavily restricted. Do not attempt to visit without proper authorization. Previous unauthorized entry via Armenia may result in an Azerbaijan entry ban.

faq.question: Can I combine Azerbaijan with Georgia?
faq.answer: Yes, an Azerbaijan-Georgia trip is very popular. Both countries offer e-visas for Indians. Direct trains and flights connect Baku and Tbilisi. Plan 5-7 days for each country.

faq.question: What are the top things to do in Baku?
faq.answer: Must-do: Old City (Icherisheher - UNESCO), Flame Towers, Heydar Aliyev Center, Highland Park, Baku Boulevard, Maiden Tower, Gobustan petroglyphs, and mud volcanoes.

faq.question: Is Azerbaijan a Muslim country?
faq.answer: Azerbaijan is a secular Muslim-majority country. It is very liberal compared to other Muslim countries. Alcohol is widely available, dress code is relaxed, and nightlife is active in Baku.

faq.question: Do I need to know Azerbaijani language?
faq.answer: English is spoken in Baku tourist areas and hotels. Russian is widely understood. Outside Baku, language can be a barrier. Download Google Translate with Azerbaijani language pack.

faq.question: Can I drive in Azerbaijan with Indian license?
faq.answer: Indian driving license is not valid in Azerbaijan. You need an International Driving Permit (IDP) to rent a car. Get one from your local RTO in India before traveling.

faq.question: What is the internet connectivity in Azerbaijan?
faq.answer: Internet connectivity is good in Baku. Buy a local SIM card (Azercell, Bakcell, or Nar) at the airport for ~₹500 with data. 4G coverage in cities, limited in remote areas.

faq.question: Is Azerbaijan expensive for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: Azerbaijan is moderately affordable. Budget: ₹3,000-4,000/day, mid-range: ₹5,000-7,000/day. Baku is pricier than other cities. Public transport and local food are cheap. Hotels vary widely.

faq.question: Can I use Uber in Azerbaijan?
faq.answer: Uber is available in Baku and works well. Bolt is also popular. Both are affordable. Taxis are cheap but agree on fare before riding or insist on meter. Avoid unmarked taxis.

canada.visaDescription: Allows you to visit Canada for tourism, visiting family/friends, or business for up to 6 months.
canada.overview: Canada has become stricter in recent years — strong financial documentation and ties to India are crucial for approval. Parents and grandparents of Canadian citizens can apply for the Super Visa, which allows stays up to 5 years per visit. Canada co-hosts the FIFA World Cup 2026 with matches in Toronto and Vancouver.

faq.question: Do Indian citizens need a visa to visit Canada?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian passport holders require a Temporary Resident Visa (TRV) also known as Visitor Visa to enter Canada. Apply through Times of Visa starting at {{price}} with all processing included. There is no visa-free or eTA option for Indian citizens.

faq.question: What is the current Canada visa processing time for Indians?
faq.answer: As of January 2026, the Canada visitor visa processing time for Indian applicants is {{processing_time}}. This has improved from 99 days earlier. Processing times vary and you should check the IRCC website for current estimates. Apply at least 4-5 months before your intended travel date.

faq.question: What is the Canada visitor visa approval rate for Indians?
faq.answer: Canada visitor visa approval rates vary based on documentation quality, financial proof, and ties to India. Business visitor applications generally have higher approval rates than tourism. Strong documentation and clear ties to India are essential for approval.

faq.question: How long can I stay in Canada on a visitor visa?
faq.answer: A Canada visitor visa allows stays of up to 6 months per visit. The exact duration is determined by the border officer at the time of entry. If you need to stay longer, you can apply for an extension from within Canada before your authorized stay expires.

faq.question: Is interview required for Canada visitor visa?
faq.answer: No, Canada does not require an in-person interview for visitor visa applications. The entire application process is online. You only need to visit a biometric center once for enrollment (fingerprints and photo).

faq.question: What is the total cost of Canada visitor visa for Indians?
faq.answer: Canada visitor visa through Times of Visa starts at {{price}} with all processing included. This covers the complete application process including document verification and submission. Optional services like courier return are available at extra cost.

faq.question: Are Canada visa fees refundable if rejected?
faq.answer: No, Canada visa fees are non-refundable regardless of the decision. If your visa is refused, the application fee is not refunded. This is why complete and accurate documentation is critical before submitting your application through Times of Visa.

faq.question: How do I pay for Canada visa application?
faq.answer: Canada visa fees are paid online during the application submission. Multiple payment methods are accepted.

faq.question: What documents are required for Canada visitor visa?
faq.answer: Required documents include: valid passport (6+ months validity), digital photograph, bank statements (6 months), ITR (3 years), employment letter with leave approval, travel itinerary, and proof of ties to India. For family visits, add invitation letter and relationship proof.

faq.question: How much bank balance is required for Canada visa?
faq.answer: There is no official minimum, but CAD $10,000 per person (approximately ₹5-6 lakhs) is recommended for tourism. The balance should be consistent over 6 months, not recently deposited. Quality of funds (regular income vs sudden deposits) matters more than absolute amount.

faq.question: Do I need to book flights before applying for Canada visa?
faq.answer: No, you should NOT book confirmed flights before visa approval. IRCC requires a travel itinerary showing your intended dates and places, but not confirmed tickets. Book only tentative/refundable reservations. Purchase actual tickets after visa approval.

faq.question: Can I apply for Canada visa online?
faq.answer: Yes, Canada visa applications are primarily online. Fill the application form, upload documents, and pay fees online. Online applications are processed faster than paper applications.

faq.question: How do I book biometrics appointment for Canada visa?
faq.answer: After submitting your online application, IRCC sends a Biometric Instruction Letter (BIL). You have 30 days from the BIL to complete biometrics at a designated visa application center.

faq.question: How long are Canada biometrics valid?
faq.answer: Canada biometrics (fingerprints and photo) are valid for 10 years. If you apply for another Canadian visa within 10 years, you may not need to provide biometrics again. Children under 14 and adults over 79 are exempt from biometrics.

faq.question: What is Canada Super Visa?
faq.answer: The Super Visa is a special long-term visitor visa for parents and grandparents of Canadian citizens or permanent residents. It allows stays of up to 5 years per visit (vs 6 months for regular visitor visa) and is valid for up to 10 years with multiple entries.

faq.question: What are the requirements for Canada Super Visa?
faq.answer: Super Visa requires: invitation from Canadian child/grandchild, proof they meet LICO income threshold, medical exam from IRCC panel physician, and medical insurance from Canadian insurer (minimum CAD $100,000 coverage, valid for 1 year). Processing takes about 132 days.

faq.question: How much does Super Visa insurance cost?
faq.answer: Super Visa medical insurance costs vary by age and coverage. For a 65-year-old, expect CAD $1,800-2,500 per year. For older applicants (70+), costs can be CAD $3,000-5,000. Insurance must be from a Canadian company with minimum CAD $100,000 coverage.

faq.question: What is LICO for Super Visa?
faq.answer: LICO (Low Income Cut-Off) is the minimum income your Canadian host must earn to sponsor a Super Visa. For a family of 2 (host + applicant), the 2024 LICO is CAD $36,576. For family of 4, it is CAD $54,594. Host must provide Notice of Assessment as proof.

faq.question: Can parents apply for Super Visa together?
faq.answer: Yes, both parents can apply for Super Visa together in one application. The LICO calculation will include both parents in the family size. For example, if host is married with 1 child and inviting both parents, family size is 5 (host, spouse, child, 2 parents).

faq.question: Do I need a visa for FIFA World Cup 2026 in Canada?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian passport holders need a Canada Visitor Visa to attend World Cup 2026 matches in Toronto or Vancouver. Canada is hosting 13 matches at 2 stadiums. Apply by January-February 2026 given the 83-day processing time.

faq.question: Which Canadian cities are hosting World Cup 2026?
faq.answer: Canada hosts FIFA World Cup 2026 matches in 2 cities: Toronto (BMO Field) and Vancouver (BC Place). Together they will host 13 matches including group stage and early knockout rounds. The tournament runs June 11 - July 19, 2026.

faq.question: Can I visit all three World Cup countries with one visa?
faq.answer: No, you need separate visas for each country. For Canada matches: Canada Visitor Visa. For USA matches: US B1/B2 visa. For Mexico: Visa required unless you have valid US, Canada, UK, Schengen, or Japan visa (then visa-free entry to Mexico). Plan accordingly.

faq.question: When should I apply for Canada visa for World Cup 2026?
faq.answer: Apply by January-February 2026. Current processing time is {{processing_time}} (nearly 3 months). The World Cup runs June 11 - July 19, 2026. Having confirmed World Cup tickets can help demonstrate genuine purpose of visit.

faq.question: Where are Canada visa application centers located in India?
faq.answer: Canada visa application centers are located in 10 cities across India: New Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Chennai, Kolkata, Hyderabad, Chandigarh, Ahmedabad, Pune, and Jalandhar. You can submit biometrics at any center regardless of where you live.

faq.question: What are Canada visa application center operating hours?
faq.answer: Canada visa application centers operate Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. They are closed on weekends and public holidays. Premium services like Prime Time appointments (additional fee) offer early morning or late evening slots for those who cannot visit during regular hours.

faq.question: What is the Premium Lounge service at visa application centers?
faq.answer: The Premium Lounge at visa application centers offers dedicated service in a comfortable lounge, priority processing, document review assistance, and complimentary refreshments. Recommended for business applicants or those wanting a more comfortable experience.

faq.question: Why do Canada visas get rejected for Indians?
faq.answer: Top reasons: 1) Insufficient funds or unstable finances, 2) Weak ties to India (no property, family, job), 3) Unclear purpose of visit, 4) No travel history, 5) Previous visa refusals, 6) Incomplete documentation. Rejection rates can be significant without strong documentation.

faq.question: Can I reapply if Canada visa is refused?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reapply immediately after refusal. However, simply resubmitting the same application will likely result in another refusal. Address the reasons mentioned in the refusal letter, strengthen weak areas, and provide additional documentation before reapplying.

faq.question: Does Canada visa rejection affect US visa?
faq.answer: A Canada visa rejection does not automatically disqualify you from US visa. However, US visa applications ask about prior refusals and you must answer honestly. One Canadian refusal is not a major issue if you can explain what changed and have strong documentation.

faq.question: Can I visit Canada with a US visa?
faq.answer: No, a US visa does not allow entry to Canada. Indian citizens need a separate Canada Visitor Visa regardless of having US, UK, or other visas. However, having prior US/UK travel history is viewed positively and strengthens your Canada application.

faq.question: Is Canada visa easy for IT professionals?
faq.answer: IT professionals generally have good approval rates due to stable employment, good salaries, and strong ties to India. Key documents: employment letter from reputable company, good salary slips, bank statements showing regular savings, and property/family ties.

faq.question: Can retired persons get Canada visa?
faq.answer: Yes, retired persons can get Canada visitor visa. Show pension statements, savings, property ownership, and health insurance. Visiting family (children/grandchildren) in Canada is a valid reason. If visiting children, a Super Visa may be more suitable.

faq.question: Can I work on Canada visitor visa?
faq.answer: No, you cannot work on a Canada visitor visa. Working without authorization is illegal and can result in deportation, ban, and future immigration issues. If you intend to work, you need a separate Work Permit.

faq.question: Can I extend my stay in Canada?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply to extend your stay from within Canada. Apply at least 30 days before your authorized stay expires using the online IRCC portal. Extension fee is CAD $100. You can stay in Canada while your extension is being processed (maintained status).

faq.question: How does multiple entry visa work?
faq.answer: A multiple entry visa allows unlimited entries to Canada until the visa expires (up to 10 years). Each visit can be up to 6 months. You can exit and re-enter Canada multiple times without applying for a new visa. Most visitor visas are issued as multiple entry.

faq.question: Do I need a visa to transit through Canada?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian citizens need a Transit Visa to transit through Canada to another country. The transit visa is free (CAD $0 application fee) but requires the regular application process. Maximum transit time is 48 hours. Apply online.

faq.question: Is Canada good for honeymoon from India?
faq.answer: Yes, Canada is a beautiful honeymoon destination with diverse options: Niagara Falls, Banff National Park, Vancouver, Quebec City, and Toronto. Best time: June-September for pleasant weather. Apply 4-5 months ahead. Newlyweds should show marriage certificate.

faq.question: Best time to visit Canada from India?
faq.answer: Best time: June to September for pleasant weather (15-25°C). July-August is peak tourist season. For winter activities (skiing), December-March is ideal. Avoid November and March-April (shoulder seasons with unpredictable weather). Apply considering 83-day processing time.

faq.question: Is Canada visa easier than US visa?
faq.answer: Canada and US visas have similar difficulty for Indians. Canada: competitive approval rate with strong docs, {{processing_time}} processing, online process, no interview. US: varies by category, 3-4 weeks processing, interview required. Both require strong financial proof and ties to India.

faq.question: Canada visitor visa vs Super Visa: which should I choose?
faq.answer: Choose Visitor Visa for: tourism, short family visits (under 6 months), business trips. Choose Super Visa if: you are parent/grandparent of Canadian citizen/PR, want to stay up to 5 years per visit, plan multiple long-term visits. Super Visa requires additional medical insurance.

faq.question: Is Canada giving visa to Indians now?
faq.answer: Yes, Canada is currently issuing visas to Indian passport holders through standard processing. Canada visa starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa with all-inclusive pricing.

faq.question: Is there a 10 year visit visa for Canada?
faq.answer: Yes, Canada offers a 10-year Temporary Resident Visa (TRV) for Indian passport holders. However, your stay per visit is limited to 6 months maximum. Canada visa starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa.

faq.question: Is it difficult for Indians to get a Canada visa?
faq.answer: Canada visa approval depends on strong documentation of finances, ties to India, and travel history rather than nationality. Processing takes 4-6 weeks with standard fees around INR 8,000-10,000 for study/work permits.

faq.question: How much does a Canada visa from India cost?
faq.answer: Canada visitor visa (TRV) costs INR 7,325 for a single entry and INR 10,725 for multiple entries.

faq.question: Are there direct flights from India to Croatia?

faq.question: What currency does Croatia use?
faq.answer: Croatia adopted the Euro (EUR) on January 1, 2023, replacing the Croatian Kuna. As of 2026, 1 Euro equals approximately ₹93-95. Cards are widely accepted in tourist areas. Carry some cash for smaller shops and markets.

faq.question: Is Croatia expensive for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: Croatia is moderately priced by European standards. Budget travelers can manage on €60-100 per day covering accommodation, food, and transport. Dubrovnik is the most expensive city. Split and Zagreb are more affordable. Eating at local konobas and using buses helps reduce costs.

faq.question: Is vegetarian food available in Croatia?
faq.answer: Vegetarian food is available but Croatia is traditionally meat and seafood heavy. Pizza, pasta, and salads are widely available. Zagreb and larger cities have dedicated vegetarian restaurants. Coastal areas offer good cheese and vegetable dishes. Apps like HappyCow help locate options.

faq.question: Is Croatia visa easy for first-time applicants?

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Croatia?
faq.answer: Best time: May-June and September-October (pleasant weather, fewer crowds). Summer (July-August) is peak season — crowded and expensive but ideal for beach holidays. Winter is quiet, with Zagreb offering Christmas markets. Dubrovnik is busy year-round.

faq.question: When did Croatia join the Schengen Area?
faq.answer: Croatia officially joined the Schengen Area on January 1, 2023. It also adopted the Euro on the same date. This means a single Schengen visa now covers Croatia along with all other 28 Schengen member countries.

faq.question: Do Indian citizens need visa for Croatia?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian citizens require a Schengen visa to visit Croatia. Since Croatia joined Schengen on January 1, 2023, the same Schengen visa process applies. See the pricing section on this page for current visa fees.

faq.question: Is 2 lakhs enough for a Croatia Schengen visa?

faq.question: Can I get a Schengen visa in 3 days?

faq.question: How long can I stay in Egypt with a tourist visa?

faq.question: Can I extend my Egypt visa while in Egypt?
faq.answer: Yes, you can extend your visa at the Mogamma building in Tahrir Square, Cairo, or at passport offices in other cities. Apply before your current visa expires. Extension fees apply.

faq.question: Is Egypt visa single or multiple entry?
faq.answer: Both single entry and multiple entry visas are available. Single entry is standard and more affordable. Multiple entry allows re-entry within the validity period.

faq.question: Can I work in Egypt on a tourist visa?
faq.answer: No, tourist visa does not permit employment. Working on a tourist visa is illegal. A separate work visa is required.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Egypt?
faq.answer: October to April is the best time to visit Egypt when temperatures are pleasant (20-30°C). Summer months (May-September) can exceed 40°C, especially in Upper Egypt (Luxor, Aswan).

faq.question: Are there direct flights from India to Egypt?
faq.answer: Direct flights from India to Cairo are limited. Most connections go through Gulf cities (Dubai, Doha, Abu Dhabi) with flight time of 8-12 hours including layover.

faq.question: What are the must-visit places in Egypt?
faq.answer: Top destinations: Pyramids of Giza and the Sphinx (Cairo), Egyptian Museum (Cairo), Luxor Temple and Valley of the Kings (Luxor), Karnak Temple, Abu Simbel (Aswan), Sharm El Sheikh (Red Sea diving), Hurghada (beaches), and Alexandria (Mediterranean coast).

faq.question: Is Egypt safe for Indian travellers?
faq.answer: Major tourist areas in Egypt (Cairo, Luxor, Aswan, Red Sea resorts) are generally safe. Take standard precautions — avoid isolated areas at night, be aware of scams at tourist sites, and use licensed guides.

faq.question: What currency is used in Egypt?
faq.answer: Egyptian Pound (EGP) is the official currency. 1 USD equals approximately 30-50 EGP. ATMs are widely available. US Dollars are accepted at many tourist sites and hotels.

faq.question: How much does a trip to Egypt cost from India?
faq.answer: Egypt is an affordable destination. Budget travellers can manage on ₹3,000-5,000 per day. Mid-range travellers spend ₹5,000-10,000 per day. A 7-day trip typically costs ₹50,000-1,50,000 including flights, accommodation, and tours.

faq.question: Do I need a guide for the Pyramids?
faq.answer: A licensed guide is recommended but not mandatory at the Pyramids. Guides provide historical context and help navigate the site. Hire only licensed guides — avoid unsolicited offers from touts.

faq.question: Is a Nile cruise worth it?
faq.answer: Yes, a Nile cruise from Luxor to Aswan (or vice versa) is one of the highlights of visiting Egypt. The 3-4 night cruise covers major temples including Edfu and Kom Ombo. Book in advance for the best rates.

faq.question: Can I use Indian credit cards in Egypt?
faq.answer: International Visa and Mastercard are accepted at hotels, larger restaurants, and malls in Cairo and tourist areas. However, many local shops, street vendors, and smaller restaurants are cash-only. ATMs are available in cities. Carry Egyptian Pounds for daily expenses.

faq.question: Is vegetarian food available in Egypt?
faq.answer: Egypt is surprisingly vegetarian-friendly. Koshari (lentils, rice, and pasta) is the national dish and fully vegetarian. Ful medames (fava beans), falafel (called taamiya in Egypt), hummus, and baba ganoush are available everywhere. Indian restaurants are also found in Cairo.

faq.answer: Yes! Your Italy Schengen visa is valid for all 29 Schengen countries. Popular combinations: Italy + Switzerland, Italy + France, Italy + Greece. Plan your main nights in Italy.

faq.question: What bank balance for Italy visa?
faq.answer: Show €50-100 per day of stay. For 10-day Italy trip: ₹50,000-₹1,00,000 minimum. Recommended total: ₹3,00,000+. Must be consistent over 6 months - avoid last-minute deposits.

faq.question: What is best time to visit Italy?
faq.answer: Best: April-June and September-October (pleasant weather, fewer crowds). Summer (July-August) is peak season - hot and crowded. Winter is good for skiing in Alps and fewer tourists.

faq.question: Is an Italy visa easy to get for Indians?
faq.answer: Italy visa approval for Indians depends on strong documentation of finances, ties to India, and travel purpose. Standard processing takes 15 days. Approval rates are generally favorable with proper documentation.

faq.question: Is 2 lakhs enough for a Schengen visa?
faq.answer: ₹2 lakhs may be insufficient for Schengen visa as Italy typically requires ₹25,000-₹30,000 monthly bank balance proof for 90 days, plus travel costs. Comprehensive financial documentation is required; exact requirements vary by individual circumstances.

faq.question: How much does an Italy visa cost in India?

faq.question: Is Italy giving work visa for Indians in 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, Italy offers work visas for Indian citizens through categories like skilled worker visas and self-employment visas. Italy visa starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa.

faq.question: Is Italy expensive for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: Italy offers good value compared to Northern Europe. Budget travellers can manage on €60-90 per day outside peak season. Rome and Venice are pricier than smaller cities like Bologna or Naples. Street food like pizza al taglio and pasta costs €5-8, making meals very affordable.

faq.question: Are there direct flights from India to Italy?
faq.answer: Yes, direct flights operate from Delhi to Rome with multiple carriers. Flight time is about 8 hours. Connecting flights via Dubai, Doha, or Istanbul often offer better prices to Milan, Venice, and Rome from multiple Indian cities.

faq.question: What currency does Italy use?
faq.answer: Italy uses the Euro (EUR). Cards are widely accepted in cities but carry cash for small shops, gelaterias, and rural areas. ATMs (Bancomat) are available everywhere. Avoid currency exchange at tourist spots — use bank ATMs for the best rates.

faq.question: Can I use Indian credit cards in Italy?
faq.answer: International Visa and Mastercard are widely accepted across Italy at hotels, restaurants, and shops. Many places accept contactless payments. Smaller trattorias and market vendors may prefer cash. Notify your Indian bank before travel to prevent transaction blocks.

faq.question: Do I need travel insurance for Italy Schengen visa?
faq.answer: Yes, travel insurance is mandatory for Italy Schengen visa. You need minimum €30,000 medical coverage valid across all Schengen countries. The policy must cover your entire trip duration including emergency hospitalization and medical repatriation.

faq.question: Can I work in Italy on a tourist visa?
faq.answer: No, working on an Italy tourist visa is illegal. Any paid employment requires a separate Italian work visa (Nulla Osta). Violating visa conditions can result in deportation and a ban from the entire Schengen area.

faq.question: What if my Italy visa application is rejected?
faq.answer: If rejected, you receive a refusal letter with specific reasons. You can reapply immediately — there is no waiting period. Address the stated issues: improve bank statements, add property documents, or strengthen your travel itinerary. Fees must be paid again.

faq.question: How can I track my Italy visa application status?
faq.answer: Times of Visa tracks your application and provides proactive updates throughout the process. You will be notified of any status changes, additional document requests, or embassy communications. No need to check yourself — we monitor daily on your behalf.

faq.question: How long does Italy visa processing take from India?
faq.answer: Standard processing for an Italy Schengen visa is {{processing_time}} from the date of submission. During peak season (May-September), it may take longer. Apply at least 30-45 days before your planned travel date to allow sufficient time.

faq.question: Can I get an Italy visa urgently from India?
faq.answer: Expedited processing may be available at an additional cost through the visa application center. However, standard processing is the recommended route for most applicants. Plan ahead and apply 4-6 weeks before travel. Italy visa starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa.

faq.question: What happens if my Italy visa processing is delayed?
faq.answer: Delays can occur during peak season or if the embassy requests additional documents. Times of Visa monitors your application daily and keeps you informed throughout. If extra documents are needed, we guide you immediately so there is no further delay.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Italy from India?
faq.answer: April to June and September to October offer the best weather for sightseeing in Italy. July-August is peak season with high prices and crowds. Winter (November-February) is quieter and cheaper, ideal for Milan shopping and ski resorts in the Dolomites.

faq.question: Is interview required for Italy Schengen visa?
faq.answer: No, Italy does not require a visa interview. You only need to visit the visa application center for biometric enrollment (fingerprints and photo). If you gave Schengen biometrics within the last 59 months, you may be exempt from visiting in person.

japan.visaDescription: Indian citizens need a tourist visa or eVISA to visit Japan. Starting {{price}} through Times of Visa. eVISA available for tourism since September 2025. Processing: 5-10 working days. No interview required. Stay up to 90 days.
japan.overview: Japan offers eVISA (tourism only, since September 2025) and traditional paper visa for Indian citizens. Starting {{price}} through Times of Visa. eVISA must be shown on your smartphone with active internet — printouts are NOT accepted. Paper visa is required for business, family visits, work, or transit. Japan values detailed planning: include a day-by-day itinerary, confirmed hotel bookings for every night, and return flight tickets.

faq.question: Does Japan have eVISA for Indians?
faq.answer: Yes! Japan launched eVISA for Indian citizens in September 2025. The eVISA is available for tourism purposes. The eVISA is received electronically and must be shown on your smartphone at the airport.

faq.question: Can I use eVISA for business trip to Japan?
faq.answer: No, Japan eVISA is ONLY for tourism. For business visits, visiting friends/family, work, study, or transit, you must apply for a traditional paper visa.

faq.question: Can Indians apply for Japan eVISA directly online?
faq.answer: Indians residing in India apply through authorized visa processing channels. Indians residing abroad may have different application options.

faq.question: What is the Japan visa fee for Indians in 2026?
faq.answer: Japan visa for Indians starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa, which includes application processing and document support. Transit visa fees are lower. Note: Japan may revise visa fees in 2026.

faq.question: Is Japan visa free for Indians?
faq.answer: No, Japan visa is not free for Indian citizens. Japan tourist visa starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa. This makes it one of the most affordable tourist visas for Indians compared to other major destinations.

faq.question: Is visa fee refundable if Japan visa is rejected?
faq.answer: Yes, the visa fee is refunded if your visa is rejected. However, the service charge is non-refundable regardless of outcome.

faq.question: What are the payment methods for Japan visa at the visa application center?
faq.answer: In New Delhi: Cash or card in INR. In Gurugram, Chandigarh, Lucknow: Separate Demand Drafts required for visa fee and service charge in favor of the visa application center

faq.question: How long does Japan visa take for Indians?
faq.answer: Japan visa processing takes {{processing_time}}. During peak seasons (cherry blossom, autumn), it may take up to 14 days. Apply at least 3 weeks before travel.

faq.question: Can I get urgent Japan visa?
faq.answer: There is no official express processing for Japan visa. Standard processing is 5-10 days. For genuine emergencies, contact the Japanese Embassy directly, but expedited processing is not guaranteed.

faq.question: When should I apply for Japan visa?
faq.answer: Apply 3-4 weeks before your travel date. You can apply maximum 3 months before travel. Applying too early means the 3-month validity may expire before your trip.

faq.question: What documents are required for Japan visa from India?
faq.answer: Required: Valid passport (6+ months validity, 2 blank pages), completed application form, photo (45x35mm), detailed day-by-day itinerary, confirmed flight and hotel bookings, 6 months bank statements, ITR (3 years), employment letter with leave approval.

faq.question: Is itinerary mandatory for Japan visa?
faq.answer: Yes, a detailed day-by-day itinerary is MANDATORY. It should include: dates, cities to visit, attractions/activities each day, hotel names with Japanese addresses, and transportation between cities. Japan takes itineraries very seriously.

faq.question: What photo size is required for Japan visa?
faq.answer: Japan visa requires 45mm x 35mm photos - this is DIFFERENT from Indian passport photos (35x35mm). Background must be white, photo taken within 6 months, no glasses, neutral expression.

faq.question: What is the minimum bank balance for Japan visa?
faq.answer: There is no official minimum, but ₹50,000-1,00,000 per person is recommended. Your balance should comfortably cover trip expenses. Show 6 months statements with regular income/salary credits.

faq.question: Do I need confirmed hotel bookings for Japan visa?
faq.answer: Yes, confirmed hotel bookings for EVERY night of your stay are required. Japan embassy wants to know exactly where you will sleep each night. Ryokans, hostels, and Airbnb with confirmation are acceptable.

faq.question: Do I need appointment for Japan visa at the visa application center?
faq.answer: Depends on city. Appointment REQUIRED: Bangalore, Chennai, Cochin, Hyderabad, Puducherry. Walk-in allowed: New Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata. Check the visa application center website for your city.

faq.question: Is interview required for Japan visa?
faq.answer: No, there is NO interview for Japan tourist visa. You submit documents at the visa application center and wait for processing. The embassy may contact you by email if additional documents are needed.

faq.question: Where to apply for Japan visa in India?
faq.answer: Check jurisdiction based on your residence state on the visa application center website.

faq.question: How long can I stay in Japan on tourist visa?
faq.answer: Japan tourist visa allows stays of 15, 30, or up to 90 days depending on visa type. First-time applicants usually get 15 days. eVISA allows up to 90 days for tourism. Duration is stamped on your visa.

faq.question: How long is Japan visa valid?
faq.answer: Single entry Japan visa is valid for 3 months from issue date - you must enter Japan within this period. Multiple entry visas can be valid for 1, 3, or 5 years.

faq.question: Can I extend Japan tourist visa?
faq.answer: The visa itself cannot be extended. However, if you hold a paper (sticker) visa, you may apply for an extension of your stay period at a Regional Immigration Bureau in Japan before your current stay expires. This is not guaranteed and is subject to approval. eVISA holders cannot extend their stay. Overstaying without approval can result in deportation, fines, and future visa bans.

faq.question: Can I work in Japan on tourist visa?
faq.answer: NO, working on tourist visa is strictly illegal in Japan. This includes part-time jobs, freelancing, or any paid activity. Violation can lead to arrest, deportation, and permanent ban.

faq.question: Can students apply for Japan tourist visa?
faq.answer: Yes, students can apply. Provide: college ID, enrollment letter, parent/guardian as financial sponsor with their bank statements and ITR, and clear travel purpose (vacation, cultural visit).

faq.question: First time applying for Japan visa - what are my chances?
faq.answer: First-time applicants have good chances (80%+) if documentation is complete. Focus on: detailed itinerary, confirmed bookings, strong financials, and clear travel purpose. Previous international travel helps but is not mandatory.

faq.question: When is cherry blossom season in Japan 2026?
faq.answer: Cherry blossom (Sakura) season 2026 is expected: Tokyo (late March-early April), Kyoto/Osaka (late March-mid April), Hokkaido (late April-early May). Peak bloom lasts only 1-2 weeks. Apply for visa 2-3 months in advance for this popular season.

faq.question: How to get Japan visa for cherry blossom season?
faq.answer: Apply at least 6-8 weeks before travel (January-February for April trip). Book refundable hotels, flights, and apply immediately. Cherry blossom season has highest visa demand. Show specific sakura viewing spots in your itinerary.

faq.question: When is autumn foliage season in Japan?
faq.answer: Autumn colors (Koyo) peak: Hokkaido (late September-October), Tokyo (mid November-early December), Kyoto (mid-late November). Less crowded than cherry blossom but equally beautiful. Apply for visa 2 months in advance.

faq.question: Best time to visit Japan from India?
faq.answer: Cherry blossom season (late March-early May) and autumn foliage (October-November) are most popular but crowded. Summer (June-August) is hot and humid. Winter (December-February) is good for skiing and fewer crowds.

faq.question: Japan visa for anime/manga fans - any special tours?
faq.answer: Include anime-themed itinerary: Akihabara (Tokyo), Ghibli Museum (Mitaka), Pokemon Center, Jump Shop. No special visa category but detailed itinerary showing fan activities strengthens your application. Book Ghibli Museum tickets in advance.

faq.question: What is Japan visa approval rate for Indians?
faq.answer: Japan visa approval rate for Indians is approximately 85-90% - one of the highest among Asian countries. Key factors: complete documentation, detailed itinerary, confirmed bookings, stable employment, and clear travel purpose.

faq.question: Why do Japan visas get rejected for Indians?
faq.answer: Common rejection reasons: 1) Incomplete/vague itinerary, 2) Insufficient funds, 3) Unconfirmed hotel/flight bookings, 4) Missing documents, 5) Suspected immigrant intent. Japan rarely rejects well-prepared applications.

faq.question: How to increase Japan visa approval chances?
faq.answer: Tips for approval: 1) Detailed day-by-day itinerary with Japanese addresses, 2) All hotel bookings confirmed, 3) 6 months bank statements with ₹50,000+ balance, 4) Complete employment proof, 5) Previous international travel history helps.

faq.question: Should I buy JR Pass for Japan trip?
faq.answer: JR Pass is worth it if traveling between multiple cities (Tokyo-Kyoto-Osaka-Hiroshima). 7-day pass costs ~₹25,000 and covers unlimited Shinkansen (bullet train). Calculate your routes on Hyperdia.com to decide if it saves money.

faq.question: Is English widely spoken in Japan?
faq.answer: English is limited outside major tourist areas. Train/airport signs are in English. Download Google Translate with offline Japanese. Learn basics: Sumimasen (excuse me), Arigatou (thank you), Eigo (English).

faq.question: Do I need cash or can I use cards in Japan?
faq.answer: Japan is still cash-heavy. Carry yen for small shops, temples, and rural areas. 7-Eleven ATMs accept international cards. Suica/Pasmo IC cards work for trains and convenience stores. Major hotels and shops accept credit cards.

faq.question: What SIM card should I use in Japan?
faq.answer: Options: 1) Pocket WiFi (₹400-600/day, unlimited), 2) Tourist SIM cards at airport (Sakura Mobile, IIJmio), 3) eSIM (Airalo, Ubigi). Japanese SIMs are data-only (no calls). Order pocket WiFi online before arrival for airport pickup.

faq.question: How much money should I budget for Japan trip?
faq.answer: Budget per day: Budget (₹5,000-8,000), Mid-range (₹10,000-15,000), Luxury (₹20,000+). Includes accommodation, food, transport, attractions. Tokyo/Kyoto are pricier. 7-day trip: ₹80,000-1,50,000 (excluding flights).

faq.question: Do Indians get a Japan visa easily?
faq.answer: Japan visa approval for Indians depends on documentation quality and financial stability. Visa application centers in major Indian cities process applications; standard tourist visas cost ₹11,500-₹16,000 with 4-5 day processing. Strong proof of funds, employment, and ties to India improve approval chances.

faq.answer: Japan does not offer a 5-year tourist visa for Indian passport holders. Indians can get a 90-day visa-free stay or apply for a Single/Multiple Entry visa valid up to 1 year, Mumbai, and other Indian cities.

faq.answer: Indian passport holders can obtain Japan tourist visas with standard documents like passport, photos, and financial proof. Processing typically takes 4-5 working days. Japan visa starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa.

malaysia.visaDescription: Indian citizens can visit Malaysia without a visa for up to 30 days for tourism purposes. No prior application required.
malaysia.overview: Malaysia is one of the easiest countries for Indians to visit — no visa, no fees, no application. Simply arrive with your passport, return ticket, and hotel booking. With a large Indian community, Tamil signage, vegetarian food everywhere, and direct flights from all major Indian cities, Malaysia feels almost like home.

faq.question: Do Indians need visa for Malaysia?
faq.answer: No, Indian citizens do not need a visa for Malaysia for tourism visits up to 30 days. You can enter visa-free at any Malaysian airport with valid passport and return ticket.

faq.question: How many days can Indians stay in Malaysia without visa?
faq.answer: Indians can stay in Malaysia for up to 30 days without a visa. This can be extended for another 30 days (total 60 days) by applying at Malaysia Immigration office.

faq.question: Is Malaysia visa-free for Indians in 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, Malaysia continues to offer visa-free entry for Indian citizens in 2026. No prior application or fee required for stays up to 30 days.

faq.question: What documents are needed for Malaysia from India?
faq.answer: For visa-free entry, you need: Valid passport (6+ months validity), confirmed return flight ticket, hotel booking or accommodation proof, and proof of sufficient funds. No visa application needed.

faq.question: Can I extend my stay in Malaysia beyond 30 days?
faq.answer: Yes, you can extend your stay by 30 more days by visiting the Immigration Department at Putrajaya. Extension fee is around MYR 100. Apply before your current stay expires.

faq.question: How much money should I carry to Malaysia?
faq.answer: Immigration recommends MYR 500 per day of stay (approx ₹9,000/day). For a 7-day trip, carry at least MYR 3,500 or equivalent. Credit/debit cards are widely accepted.

faq.question: Can I work in Malaysia on visa-free entry?
faq.answer: No, visa-free entry is only for tourism. Working is strictly prohibited. For employment, you need a proper work permit/visa sponsored by a Malaysian employer.

faq.question: Is travel insurance required for Malaysia?
faq.answer: Travel insurance is not mandatory for Malaysia entry, but highly recommended. Medical costs can be high. Get coverage of at least USD 50,000.

faq.question: Which airports in Malaysia allow visa-free entry for Indians?
faq.answer: All Malaysian international airports allow visa-free entry: KLIA, KLIA2, Penang, Langkawi, Kota Kinabalu, Kuching, and others. Land borders also allow visa-free entry.

faq.question: What is the difference between eVisa and visa-free entry to Malaysia?
faq.answer: Visa-free entry requires no application - just arrive with documents. eVisa requires online application (USD 25) but provides pre-approval. For most Indian tourists, visa-free entry is sufficient.

faq.question: Can I enter Malaysia multiple times on visa-free?
faq.answer: Yes, you can enter Malaysia multiple times using visa-free entry. Each entry allows 30-day stay. However, frequent entries may raise questions at immigration about your purpose.

faq.question: Is return ticket mandatory for Malaysia?
faq.answer: Yes, a confirmed return or onward ticket is practically mandatory. Airlines may deny boarding and immigration may deny entry without proof of departure from Malaysia.

faq.question: Can I transit through Malaysia without visa?
faq.answer: Yes, Indians can transit through Malaysia without visa. If staying in transit area (airside), no entry stamp needed. If exiting airport, you get visa-free entry stamp.

faq.question: What happens if I overstay in Malaysia?
faq.answer: Overstaying is a serious offense. Penalties include fines (MYR 500+), detention, deportation, and ban from re-entering Malaysia. Always leave before your permitted stay expires.

faq.question: Is Malaysia safe for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: Yes, Malaysia is very safe for Indian tourists. Large Indian community, familiar food, Tamil signage in many areas. Crime rate is low in tourist areas. Take normal precautions.

faq.question: Best time to visit Malaysia from India?
faq.answer: Malaysia is good year-round due to tropical climate. Best months: March-October for west coast (KL, Langkawi), April-September for east coast (islands). Avoid monsoon seasons.

faq.question: How long is flight from India to Malaysia?
faq.answer: Direct flights: Delhi to KL is about 5.5 hours, Mumbai to KL is about 5 hours, Chennai to KL is about 4 hours. Multiple daily flights available from major Indian cities.

faq.question: Do I need yellow fever vaccination for Malaysia?
faq.answer: Yellow fever vaccination is required only if arriving from a yellow fever endemic country. Not required for direct travel from India. Carry certificate if you have transit in Africa/South America.

faq.question: Can I visit Singapore from Malaysia on same trip?
faq.answer: Yes, Singapore is just across the border. Indians need eVisa for Singapore. Many tourists combine Malaysia-Singapore trips. Bus/train available from KL to Singapore (5-6 hours).

faq.question: Is vegetarian food available in Malaysia?
faq.answer: Yes, vegetarian food is widely available in Malaysia due to large Indian, Chinese Buddhist, and local vegan communities. Indian restaurants, mamak stalls, and Chinese vegetarian restaurants are common.

faq.question: Can I use Indian Rupees in Malaysia?
faq.answer: No, Malaysian Ringgit (MYR) is the only accepted currency. Exchange money at KLIA airport (decent rates) or money changers in KL. Credit/debit cards widely accepted. ATMs available everywhere.

faq.question: Is Uber available in Malaysia?
faq.answer: No, Uber exited Malaysia. Use Grab app instead - it works for rides, food delivery, and payments. Download before arrival. Very reliable and affordable.

faq.question: Do I need adapter for Indian plugs in Malaysia?
faq.answer: Malaysia uses Type G plugs (UK-style 3-pin). Indian plugs (Type D) will not fit. Bring a universal adapter or buy one at any Malaysian mall cheaply.

faq.question: Is English spoken in Malaysia?
faq.answer: Yes, English is widely spoken in Malaysia, especially in tourist areas, hotels, and shopping malls. Many Malaysians are multilingual. Communication is easy for Indians.

faq.question: What is the time difference between India and Malaysia?
faq.answer: Malaysia is 2.5 hours ahead of India. When it is 12:00 PM in India, it is 2:30 PM in Malaysia. Remember this when booking flights and making calls.

faq.question: Can I drink tap water in Malaysia?
faq.answer: Tap water in Malaysia is generally safe but bottled water is recommended for tourists. Bottled water is cheap and available everywhere. Hotels often provide free bottled water.

faq.question: What should I wear in Malaysia?
faq.answer: Dress modestly, especially at religious sites. Casual clothes fine for tourist areas. Cover knees and shoulders when visiting mosques. Lightweight, breathable fabrics recommended due to heat.

faq.question: Is Malaysia expensive for Indians?
faq.answer: Malaysia is moderately affordable. Budget: ₹3,000-5,000/day. Mid-range: ₹5,000-10,000/day. Street food is cheap. Accommodation varies widely. Langkawi duty-free makes shopping affordable.

faq.question: Can I get Indian SIM card working in Malaysia?
faq.answer: International roaming is expensive. Better to buy local SIM at airport (Hotlink, Digi, Celcom). Costs MYR 30-50 for tourist package with data. Just need passport to purchase.

faq.question: How much does a Malaysia visa cost from India?
faq.answer: Malaysia is visa-free for Indian passport holders for stays up to 30 days — no visa fee needed. For travel packages and assistance, Malaysia travel starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa.

faq.question: What are common reasons for Malaysia visa rejections?
faq.answer: Common Malaysia visa rejections for Indian passport holders include incomplete documents, insufficient funds (bank statements showing less than ₹2-3 lakhs), employment letter issues, and invalid travel history. Submit complete forms, Mumbai, and Bangalore to avoid delays and rejections.

faq.question: Is Malaysia a tourist visa for 3 months?
faq.answer: Malaysia offers a 90-day tourist visa (eVisa or on-arrival) for Indian passport holders. The eVisa costs around ₹1,500-2,000 and can be applied online or.

faq.question: What is the minimum bank balance for Malaysia visa?
faq.answer: Malaysia does not mandate a specific minimum bank balance for tourist visas. However, banks typically recommend INR 1-2 lakhs as proof of financial capability. Requirements may vary by visa officer discretion based on your profile.

faq.question: Is Malaysia visa free for Indian citizens?
faq.answer: No, Indian citizens require a visa for Malaysia. A single-entry tourist visa costs around INR 1,500-2,000 and is typically valid for 3 months.

maldives.visaDescription: Indian citizens receive a FREE Visa on Arrival in Maldives for up to 30 days. No fees, no prior application required.
maldives.overview: The Maldives offers free visa on arrival to Indian citizens — the easiest entry process for one of the world

faq.question: Do Indians need visa for Maldives?
faq.answer: Indians get FREE Visa on Arrival in Maldives. No prior application or fee required. Just arrive with passport, hotel booking, and return ticket. 30 days stay granted instantly.

faq.question: What is the visa fee for Maldives from India?
faq.answer: There is NO visa fee for Maldives. The tourist visa on arrival is completely FREE for all nationalities including Indians. You only pay for flights, hotels, and experiences.

faq.question: How many days can Indians stay in Maldives?
faq.answer: Indians can stay up to 30 days on the free visa on arrival. Extension possible for another 60 days (total 90 days) by applying at Maldives Immigration (fee: USD 750).

faq.question: Is hotel booking mandatory for Maldives visa?
faq.answer: Yes, hotel/resort booking is MANDATORY. Immigration will check your accommodation confirmation. You cannot enter Maldives without pre-booked accommodation.

faq.question: Can I book Maldives hotel after reaching?
faq.answer: No, you must have hotel booking BEFORE arrival. Immigration checks booking at entry. Book in advance - even a refundable booking works.

faq.question: How long is flight from India to Maldives?
faq.answer: Direct flights: Mumbai to Malé is 3.5 hours, Delhi to Malé is 4.5 hours, Chennai/Bangalore to Malé is 2-2.5 hours. Multiple daily flights available from major Indian cities.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Maldives?
faq.answer: Best time is dry season: November to April. Peak season is December-January (Christmas/New Year). May-October has more rain but cheaper rates.

faq.question: Is Maldives expensive for Indians?
faq.answer: Maldives can be expensive - resorts range from USD 200-2000/night. Budget options: Local island guesthouses (USD 50-100), off-season travel, all-inclusive packages.

faq.question: Can I drink alcohol in Maldives?
faq.answer: Alcohol is available ONLY at resorts (they have special licenses). Alcohol is banned in Malé and local islands. Do not import alcohol - it will be confiscated.

faq.question: Do I need travel insurance for Maldives?
faq.answer: Travel insurance is not mandatory but highly recommended. Medical facilities are limited on resort islands. Evacuation to Malé or abroad can be expensive.

faq.question: What currency is used in Maldives?
faq.answer: Maldivian Rufiyaa (MVR) is local currency, but USD is widely accepted everywhere, especially at resorts. Credit cards accepted at most resorts. Carry some cash for tips.

faq.question: How to reach resort from Malé airport?
faq.answer: Resorts arrange transfers: Speedboat (nearby islands, 15-60 mins), Seaplane (distant islands, 30-60 mins), or Domestic flight + speedboat. Cost: USD 50-500+ depending on distance.

faq.question: Can I visit local islands in Maldives?
faq.answer: Yes, you can visit local islands for cultural experience. Book day trips from resort. Local islands have stricter dress codes and no alcohol. Great for budget travelers.

faq.question: Is vegetarian food available in Maldives?
faq.answer: Yes, all resorts offer vegetarian options. Indian restaurants available in Malé. Most resorts accommodate dietary requirements - inform them in advance.

faq.question: What is the passport validity requirement for Maldives?
faq.answer: Maldives requires passport valid for at least 1 month from entry date. This is one of the most relaxed requirements globally. Most countries require 6 months.

faq.question: Can I combine Maldives with Sri Lanka trip?
faq.answer: Yes, popular combination! Colombo is just 1.5 hours from Malé. Many travelers do Sri Lanka + Maldives. Indians need eVisa for Sri Lanka.

faq.question: What is the time difference between India and Maldives?
faq.answer: Maldives is 30 minutes behind India (IST). When it is 12:00 PM in India, it is 11:30 AM in Maldives. Easy time zone adjustment.

faq.question: Do I need adapter for Indian plugs in Maldives?
faq.answer: Maldives uses Type G (UK-style 3-pin) and Type D (Indian) plugs. Most resorts have universal outlets. Carry adapter just in case.

faq.question: Is WiFi available in Maldives resorts?
faq.answer: Yes, all resorts have WiFi but quality varies. Some charge extra for high-speed. Local SIM with data is cheaper alternative. Dhiraagu and Ooredoo are main operators.

faq.question: What is included in all-inclusive Maldives packages?
faq.answer: All-inclusive typically covers: Accommodation, all meals, selected drinks (may exclude premium alcohol), some activities. Water sports and spa usually extra.

faq.question: Can I island hop in Maldives?
faq.answer: Yes, island hopping is possible via speedboat or public ferry. Local ferries are cheap but slow. Speedboats are faster but expensive. Plan transfers in advance.

faq.question: Is snorkeling good for beginners in Maldives?
faq.answer: Yes, Maldives is perfect for beginners! House coral formations at resorts are shallow and calm. Equipment usually free at resorts. Marine life visible within meters of beach.

faq.question: What should I pack for Maldives?
faq.answer: Pack: Sunscreen (reef-safe), swimwear, light clothes, underwater camera, waterproof phone case, insect repellent, prescription medicines. Resorts are casual - no formal wear needed.

faq.question: Is it safe to swim in Maldives?
faq.answer: Yes, very safe within resort lagoons. Be cautious of currents outside reef. Most resorts have designated swimming areas. Follow resort safety guidelines. Jellyfish occasionally present.

faq.question: Can I see dolphins in Maldives?
faq.answer: Yes! Dolphin cruises are popular activity. Spinner dolphins are common. Many resorts include dolphin watching in excursions. Best time is sunrise or sunset.

faq.question: What is the average resort cost per night in Maldives?
faq.answer: Budget (local islands): $50-150/night. Mid-range: $300-600/night. Luxury: $800-2000/night. Ultra-luxury: $2000-5000+/night. Prices vary by season and inclusions.

faq.question: Is Maldives good for families with kids?
faq.answer: Yes, many resorts are family-friendly with kids clubs, shallow lagoons, and family villas. Some resorts are adults-only - check before booking. Activities available for all ages.

faq.question: How much does a Maldives visa cost for Indians?
faq.answer: Maldives offers visa-free entry for Indian passport holders for up to 30 days. No visa fee is charged. Indians can arrive directly at Malé airport without prior visa application.

faq.question: How to get Maldives visa on arrival?
faq.answer: Indian passport holders get 30-day visa-on-arrival (VOA) free at Velana International Airport, Male. Bring your passport, return ticket, and proof of accommodation. No prior application needed.

faq.question: Does Maldives stamp a passport?
faq.answer: Yes, Maldives stamps passports on arrival. Indian citizens receive an On-Arrival Visa stamp in their passport at Velana International Airport (Male). Keep your passport safe as the stamp serves as your entry/exit record for the duration of your stay.

faq.question: Is Maldives visa on arrival?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian passport holders get 30 days visa-free entry to Maldives on arrival. No advance visa application needed — you receive it directly at Malé airport upon landing.

faq.question: Is Maldives open for Indians?
faq.answer: Yes, Maldives is open for Indian passport holders. Indians get a FREE 30-day tourist visa on arrival — no advance application needed. Just carry your passport, return flight ticket, and hotel booking confirmation.
