mexico.visaDescription: Required for Indian passport holders visiting Mexico for tourism, business meetings, or transit. Valid US, Canada, Japan, UK, or Schengen visa holders are EXEMPT and can enter visa-free for up to 180 days.
mexico.overview: If you hold a valid visa from USA, Canada, UK, Schengen, or Japan, you do NOT need a Mexico visa — enter visa-free for up to 180 days. For those without these visas, the application requires an in-person interview at the Embassy in New Delhi. CITAS appointments cannot be rescheduled once confirmed, so plan carefully. Mexico co-hosts the FIFA World Cup 2026 with matches in Mexico City, Guadalajara, and Monterrey.

mandatory.details: Minimum 6 months validity beyond intended stay. At least 2 blank pages. Carry old passports if available.
Downloaded from embassy website. Completed and signed.
2 recent color photos (3.5 x 4.5 cm), white background, no glasses, face fully visible.
Proof of payment from designated bank (RBL for Delhi, HDFC for Mumbai).
Printed confirmation of your scheduled appointment.
Round-trip flight booking showing entry and exit from Mexico.
Confirmed accommodation for entire stay in Mexico.

financial.details: Last 3-6 months from savings account. Show ₹70,000-1,00,000+ balance. Stamped by bank.
Last 2-3 years ITR acknowledgment with computation.
Last 3 months (if employed).

employment.details: From employer stating designation, salary, tenure, and approved leave dates. On company letterhead.
For self-employed: GST certificate, company incorporation documents, business ITR.
For students: College ID, enrollment letter, leave approval, parents\

additional.details: Not mandatory but highly recommended. Coverage for medical emergencies.
Explaining purpose of visit, itinerary, and ties to India. Recommended but not mandatory.
If visiting family/friends in Mexico, include their invitation with ID and address.

faq.question: Do Indians need a visa for Mexico?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian passport holders need a visa for Mexico UNLESS they have a valid visa from USA, Canada, UK, Schengen, or Japan. With any of these valid visas, you can enter Mexico visa-free for up to 180 days.

faq.question: Can I visit Mexico with a US visa?
faq.answer: Yes! Indians with a valid US visa (B1/B2, H1B, L1, F1, or any other valid US visa) can enter Mexico WITHOUT a separate Mexican visa. You can stay up to 180 days for tourism, business, or transit.

faq.question: Can I enter Mexico with an expired US visa?
faq.answer: No, your US visa must be VALID at the time of entry into Mexico and for your entire stay. Expired visas do not qualify for visa-free entry. You would need to apply for a Mexico visa separately.

faq.question: Does US tourist visa stamp work for Mexico entry?
faq.answer: Yes, any valid US visa stamped in your passport (B1/B2, H1B, L1, F1, etc.) allows visa-free entry to Mexico. The visa must be physically stamped - not just approved. I-20 or temporary documents alone do NOT work.

faq.question: Can I enter Mexico with Schengen visa?
faq.answer: Yes! Indians with valid Schengen visa can enter Mexico visa-free for up to 180 days. Same applies to valid UK, Canada, and Japan visas.

faq.question: What visas allow visa-free entry to Mexico for Indians?
faq.answer: Indians can enter Mexico visa-free with valid visas from: USA (any type), Canada (visitor visa or PR), UK (any type), Schengen (any type), or Japan (any type). Permanent Residence cards from these countries also work.

faq.question: What is Mexico visa fee for Indians?
faq.answer: Mexico visa for Indians starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa, which includes application processing, document verification, and expert support. Book online above for current pricing and visa plans.

faq.question: Where do I pay Mexico visa fee in India?
faq.answer: For New Delhi applicants: Pay at RBL Bank. For Mumbai applicants: Pay at HDFC Bank. Keep the payment receipt - it is required at your embassy appointment.

faq.question: Is Mexico visa fee refundable if rejected?
faq.answer: No, the Mexico visa fee is non-refundable regardless of the outcome. Make sure your documents are complete before applying.

faq.question: How long does Mexico visa take for Indians?
faq.answer: Mexico visa processing is fast - typically {{processing_time}}. Same-day issuance is possible if all documents are complete. This is one of the fastest processing times among major countries.

faq.question: Can I get Mexico visa same day?
faq.answer: Yes, same-day visa issuance is possible at the Mexico Embassy if your documents are complete and in order. However, standard processing is {{processing_time}}.

faq.question: When should I apply for Mexico visa?
faq.answer: Apply 2-4 weeks before your travel date. Appointment slots are limited, so book early. For World Cup 2026, apply at least 2-3 months in advance.

faq.question: How can I track my Mexico visa application status?
faq.answer: Times of Visa tracks your application and provides proactive updates throughout the process. You will be notified of any status changes or additional document requests. No need to check yourself — we monitor on your behalf.

faq.question: What happens if my Mexico visa processing is delayed?
faq.answer: Processing delays can occur during peak travel season or World Cup 2026 demand. Times of Visa monitors your application and keeps you informed. If the embassy requests additional documents, we guide you immediately.

faq.question: Is interview required for Mexico visa?
faq.answer: Yes, an in-person interview at the Embassy of Mexico in New Delhi is required. Biometrics (photo and fingerprints) are also captured during the appointment.

faq.question: How do I book Mexico visa appointment?
faq.answer: Book your appointment online. IMPORTANT: Once booked, appointments CANNOT be rescheduled. If you miss it, you must book a completely new appointment.

faq.question: Can I reschedule my Mexico visa appointment?
faq.answer: No, rescheduling is not allowed. If you cannot attend your scheduled appointment, you must book a brand new appointment through CITAS.

faq.question: What happens at Mexico visa interview?
faq.answer: The interview is brief (5-10 minutes). The officer will verify your documents, ask about your travel purpose and itinerary, and capture your biometrics (photo and fingerprints). Be clear and honest about your travel plans.

faq.question: Where is Mexico Embassy in India?
faq.answer: Embassy of Mexico is at C-8, Benito Juarez Marg, Block C, Anand Niketan, New Delhi 110021. Phone: +91-11-2411-7180. There are Honorary Consulates in Mumbai and Bengaluru with limited services.

faq.question: What documents are needed for Mexico visa?
faq.answer: Required: Valid passport (6+ months), photos (3.5x4.5cm, white background), bank statements (3-6 months), ITR (2-3 years), employment letter, flight booking, hotel reservation, visa fee receipt, CITAS appointment confirmation.

faq.question: What is minimum bank balance for Mexico visa?
faq.answer: Recommended bank balance is ₹70,000-1,00,000 per person. Show consistent balance over 3-6 months - avoid sudden large deposits. Include ITR and salary slips for income proof.

faq.question: What photo size is needed for Mexico visa?
faq.answer: Passport size photos: 3.5 x 4.5 cm, white background, no glasses, neutral expression, face fully visible. Photos must be recent (within 6 months) and not used for any previous visa.

faq.question: How long is Mexico visa valid?
faq.answer: Mexico tourist visa is typically valid for up to 10 years with multiple entries. Actual validity depends on your passport validity and applicant profile.

faq.question: How long can I stay in Mexico with tourist visa?
faq.answer: Maximum stay is 180 days per visit. However, the immigration officer at entry decides your exact permitted stay - it can be anywhere from 7 to 180 days based on your documents and stated purpose.

faq.question: Can I extend Mexico tourist visa?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply for extension at Instituto Nacional de Migración (INM) office in Mexico before your permitted stay expires. Extensions are not guaranteed.

faq.question: Can I attend FIFA World Cup 2026 in Mexico?
faq.answer: Yes! Mexico is hosting World Cup 2026 matches in Mexico City, Guadalajara, and Monterrey. If you have a valid US or Canada visa, you can enter Mexico visa-free. Otherwise, apply for Mexico tourist visa.

faq.question: Which Mexican cities are hosting World Cup 2026?
faq.answer: Mexico hosts 13 World Cup 2026 matches in 3 cities: Mexico City (Estadio Azteca - 87,000 capacity), Guadalajara (Estadio Akron - 49,000), and Monterrey (Estadio BBVA - 53,000).

faq.question: Do I need Mexico visa for World Cup if I have US visa?
faq.answer: No! Indians with valid US visa can enter Mexico visa-free for World Cup 2026. This makes Mexico the easiest World Cup host country to visit for Indians who already have US visas.

faq.question: How to visit all 3 World Cup 2026 countries?
faq.answer: Get US B1/B2 visa first (required for USA). With valid US visa, enter Mexico visa-free. Apply separately for Canada visitor visa. This allows you to watch matches in USA, Mexico, and Canada.

faq.question: First time applying for Mexico visa - what are my chances?
faq.answer: Good chances (85-90% approval) with proper documentation. Focus on: clear itinerary, strong financials (₹70,000+), employment proof, and confident interview. Previous travel history helps but not mandatory.

faq.question: Can I work in Mexico on tourist visa?
faq.answer: No, tourist visa does not permit paid work in Mexico. For work, you need a separate work visa (Residente Temporal) with job offer from Mexican employer.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Mexico?
faq.answer: Best time: November-April (dry season). For beaches: December-April. Avoid hurricane season (June-November) on Caribbean coast. For World Cup 2026 (June-July): expect hot weather and peak tourist crowds.

faq.question: Is English spoken in Mexico?
faq.answer: English is widely spoken in tourist areas (Cancun, resorts, major cities). Outside tourist zones, Spanish is dominant. Learning basic Spanish phrases greatly improves your experience. Google Translate helps.

faq.question: What is Mexico visa approval rate for Indians?
faq.answer: Mexico visa approval rate for Indians is approximately 85-90% - quite high compared to other countries. Main rejection reasons: insufficient funds, incomplete documents, unclear travel purpose.

faq.question: Why do Mexico visas get rejected?
faq.answer: Common reasons: Insufficient bank balance, incomplete documents at interview, unclear travel purpose, weak ties to India. Mexico has good approval rates - rejections are usually due to documentation issues.

faq.question: Can Indians go to Mexico with a US visa?
faq.answer: No, a US visa does not permit entry to Mexico. Indian passport holders need a separate Mexican visa or qualify for visa-free entry. Apply.

faq.question: Is a Mexico visa free entry?
faq.answer: Indian passport holders get 180 days visa-free entry to Mexico as tourists. No visa fee applies, but you need a valid passport and completed immigration form (FMM).

faq.question: How much is the visa fee for Mexico from India?
faq.answer: Mexico visa for Indians starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa, which includes visa fee, document verification, and expert support. Book online above for current pricing.

faq.question: Do Indians get a visa on arrival?
faq.answer: No, Indians cannot get a visa on arrival in Mexico. Indian passport holders must obtain a Mexican visa before travel.

nepal.visaName: Nepal Visa-Free Entry for Indians (Bilateral Treaty)
nepal.visaDescription: Indian citizens do not need a visa to visit Nepal under the India-Nepal Treaty of Peace and Friendship (1950). Indians can enter, stay, and work in Nepal freely. Entry is allowed with a valid passport or even Aadhaar card at land borders.
nepal.overview: Nepal is the easiest international destination for Indian citizens. Under the India-Nepal Treaty of Peace and Friendship (1950), Indians can enter, stay, work, and live in Nepal without any visa. There is no fee, no application, no limit on stay, and no restriction on purpose. Indians can enter with just a passport (for flights) or Aadhaar card (at land borders). Nepal offers stunning Himalayan landscapes, ancient temples, and warm hospitality.

faq.question: Do Indians need a visa for Nepal?
faq.answer: No, Indians do NOT need a visa for Nepal. Under the India-Nepal Treaty of Peace and Friendship (1950), Indian citizens can enter, stay, work, and live in Nepal freely without any visa.

faq.question: Is there any fee for Indians entering Nepal?
faq.answer: Absolutely no fee. Entry to Nepal is completely free for Indian citizens. No visa fee, no entry fee, no tourist tax. Just carry a valid ID and enter.

faq.question: Do Indians need to pay airport tax in Nepal?
faq.answer: Departure tax at Tribhuvan International Airport is usually included in your airline ticket. Indians do not pay any additional visa or immigration fees.

faq.question: Is Nepal the cheapest international trip for Indians?
faq.answer: Yes, Nepal is one of the cheapest international destinations. No visa cost, Indian currency accepted, budget accommodation from ₹500/night, meals from ₹200. A 5-day trip can cost ₹15,000-25,000 including transport.

faq.question: How much money should I carry to Nepal?
faq.answer: Budget ₹2,000-4,000 per day. Carry ₹100 and lower denomination notes as larger notes are not accepted. ATMs available in cities. Credit cards accepted in hotels and restaurants in Kathmandu and Pokhara.

faq.question: What documents do Indians need for Nepal?
faq.answer: For flights: Valid Indian passport. For land borders: Aadhaar card, voter ID, or passport. No visa application, no forms, no photographs needed. The simplest international travel process.

faq.question: Can I enter Nepal with Aadhaar card?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian citizens can enter Nepal at land borders using Aadhaar card. However, for air travel (flights to Kathmandu), a valid passport is mandatory as per airline requirements.

faq.question: Do I get an entry stamp in Nepal?
faq.answer: No, Indian citizens do not receive an immigration entry/exit stamp in their passport when entering Nepal. The process is seamless with no formal immigration procedure.

faq.question: Which land borders can Indians use for Nepal?
faq.answer: Major India-Nepal border crossings: Raxaul-Birgunj (Bihar), Sunauli-Bhairahawa (UP), Kakarbhitta (from Siliguri), Nepalganj-Rupaidiha (UP), Mahendranagar-Banbasa (Uttarakhand). All open to Indians.

faq.question: Can I drive to Nepal from India?
faq.answer: Yes, Indians can drive to Nepal. Indian driving license is valid in Nepal. Major road routes: Delhi to Kathmandu via Sunauli (~1,200 km), Patna to Kathmandu via Raxaul (~400 km). Carry vehicle registration and insurance.

faq.question: Is passport mandatory for Nepal travel?
faq.answer: Passport is mandatory only for air travel to Nepal. At land borders, you can enter with Aadhaar card, voter ID, or any government photo ID. However, passport is recommended for better identification.

faq.question: Do children need passport for Nepal?
faq.answer: Children need passport for flights to Nepal. At land borders, children can enter with Aadhaar card or birth certificate. It is recommended to carry birth certificate regardless of travel mode.

faq.question: Is travel insurance needed for Nepal?
faq.answer: Travel insurance is not required but strongly recommended, especially for trekking and adventure activities. Mountain rescue and helicopter evacuation insurance is essential for treks above 3,000m.

faq.question: Do I need return ticket for Nepal?
faq.answer: No, there is no requirement for a return ticket. Indians can stay in Nepal as long as they want under the bilateral treaty. One-way tickets are perfectly fine.

faq.question: Do I need hotel booking for Nepal?
faq.answer: No, hotel booking is not required for entry. You can find accommodation on arrival. In tourist areas like Thamel (Kathmandu) and Lakeside (Pokhara), walk-in hotels are available from ₹500/night.

faq.question: What ID proofs work at Nepal border?
faq.answer: Accepted at land borders: Aadhaar card, voter ID, passport, government photo ID. Passport is the most reliable and universally accepted. Digital Aadhaar (mAadhaar) may not be accepted - carry physical copy.

faq.question: How long can Indians stay in Nepal?
faq.answer: There is NO limit on how long Indian citizens can stay in Nepal. Under the bilateral treaty, Indians can stay indefinitely - weeks, months, or years without any visa or permit.

faq.question: Can Indians work in Nepal without a visa?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian citizens can work in Nepal without a work visa under the bilateral treaty. However, some employers may require specific documentation. Government and security jobs may have restrictions.

faq.question: Can Indians study in Nepal?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian citizens can study in Nepal without a student visa. Many Indian students attend medical, engineering, and other colleges in Nepal. Direct admission process with Indian credentials.

faq.question: Can Indians buy property in Nepal?
faq.answer: Indian citizens cannot buy land or property in Nepal as per Nepali law. However, Indians can rent property and live in Nepal freely. Some investment options may be available through registered companies.

faq.question: Do Indians need permit for trekking in Nepal?
faq.answer: Yes, trekking permits (TIMS card) are required for most trekking routes regardless of nationality. Cost: ~$20 per trek. Arranged through trekking agencies or tourism board office in Kathmandu.

faq.question: Can I enter Nepal without any document?
faq.answer: You need at least one valid ID: passport (for flights) or Aadhaar/voter ID (for land borders). While the treaty allows free movement, identification is still needed for security purposes.

faq.question: Are there direct flights from India to Nepal?
faq.answer: Yes, direct flights from Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Varanasi, Bengaluru, and other cities to Kathmandu. Airlines: Air India, IndiGo, SpiceJet, Vistara, Nepal Airlines, Buddha Air. Flight time: 1.5-3 hours.

faq.question: Is Nepal safe for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: Yes, Nepal is very safe and welcoming for Indian tourists. Cultural similarities, language overlap (Hindi widely understood), and large Indian presence make it extremely comfortable.

faq.question: Best time to visit Nepal?
faq.answer: October-November (autumn) is the best time - clear skies, perfect weather, and festival season (Dashain, Tihar). March-April is second best for trekking and wildlife. Avoid monsoon (June-September).

faq.question: Can I use Indian Rupee in Nepal?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian Rupee is widely accepted in Nepal. Exchange rate: 1 INR = 1.6 NPR. IMPORTANT: Notes of ₹200, ₹500, and ₹2,000 denomination are NOT accepted. Carry ₹100 and smaller notes.

faq.question: Does Indian mobile work in Nepal?
faq.answer: Indian SIM cards work near the border but not reliably in central Nepal. Get a Nepali SIM (NTC or Ncell) at the airport or in Thamel for ₹200-500 with data. Wi-Fi available in hotels and cafes.

faq.question: What are the top things to do in Nepal?
faq.answer: Must-do: Everest Base Camp trek, Annapurna Circuit, Pokhara lakeside, Pashupatinath Temple, Boudhanath Stupa, Chitwan jungle safari, Lumbini (Buddha birthplace), paragliding in Pokhara, bungee jumping.

faq.question: How much does Nepal trekking cost for Indians?
faq.answer: Budget trek (self-guided): ₹2,000-3,000/day. Guided trek with porter: ₹4,000-6,000/day. Everest Base Camp trek (14 days): ₹50,000-1,00,000 total. Trekking permits extra (~₹1,500-2,000).

faq.question: Is vegetarian food available in Nepal?
faq.answer: Yes, vegetarian food is widely available. Dal Bhat (rice and lentils) is the national dish and often vegetarian. Tourist areas have Indian, Italian, and continental restaurants with veg options.

faq.question: Can I do Everest Base Camp trek as a beginner?
faq.answer: Yes, Everest Base Camp trek does not require mountaineering skills. It is a challenging but doable trek for fit individuals. Requires 12-14 days, basic fitness, and proper acclimatization. Hire a guide for safety.

faq.question: What altitude sickness precautions for Nepal?
faq.answer: Above 3,000m: ascend slowly (max 500m/day), stay hydrated, avoid alcohol, carry Diamox (consult doctor), know symptoms (headache, nausea, dizziness). Descend immediately if symptoms worsen.

faq.question: Can I cross from Nepal to Tibet/China?
faq.answer: Yes, but you need a Chinese visa and Tibet permit (arranged through tour agency). Indians cannot enter Tibet independently - must join an organized tour. Popular route: Kathmandu to Lhasa.

faq.question: Can I use UPI payments in Nepal?
faq.answer: UPI is not widely accepted in Nepal yet. Carry Indian Rupee cash (₹100 and smaller notes), withdraw Nepali Rupees from ATMs, or use international cards at hotels and restaurants in tourist areas.

faq.question: Is Nepal good for honeymoon?
faq.answer: Yes, Nepal is a popular honeymoon destination. Pokhara lakeside, Nagarkot sunrise views, jungle resort in Chitwan, and boutique hotels in Kathmandu offer romantic experiences at affordable prices.

faq.question: What are the festivals in Nepal?
faq.answer: Major festivals: Dashain (Oct - biggest festival), Tihar/Deepawali (Nov - festival of lights), Holi (Mar), Indra Jatra (Sep), Buddha Jayanti (May). Festivals bring colorful celebrations and local cultural experiences.

faq.question: Can I volunteer in Nepal as an Indian?
faq.answer: Yes, Indians can volunteer in Nepal without any special visa. Many NGOs in Kathmandu, Pokhara, and rural areas accept volunteers. No work permit needed for Indian citizens under bilateral treaty.

faq.question: How to get from Kathmandu to Pokhara?
faq.answer: Options: Tourist bus (6-7 hours, ₹500-1,000), domestic flight (25 minutes, ₹3,000-5,000), private car (5-6 hours, ₹5,000-8,000). Tourist bus is the most popular option for budget travelers.

faq.question: Is Nepali food similar to Indian food?
faq.answer: Nepali food has similarities to North Indian cuisine but is distinct. Dal Bhat (rice, lentils, vegetables) is the staple. Momos (dumplings) are extremely popular. Indian restaurants are widely available in tourist areas.

newzealand.visaDescription: Indian citizens need a Visitor Visa (eVisa) to enter New Zealand. Starting {{price}} through Times of Visa. Processed in 2-3 weeks. Stay up to 9 months for tourism, family visits, business, or short-term study.
newzealand.overview: Indian citizens must provide a chest X-ray from an Immigration NZ-approved physician (mandatory regardless of stay duration). From December 2025, Police Clearance Certificates are only accepted from Regional Passport Offices — local police station PCCs are no longer valid. faq.question: What is the New Zealand visa fee for Indians in 2026?
faq.answer: The New Zealand visitor visa fee for Indians includes the base visa fee plus the International Visitor Conservation and Tourism Levy (IVL). Starting {{price}} when you book through Times of Visa, which covers visa fee, IVL, and processing support.

faq.question: Is New Zealand visa fee refundable if rejected?
faq.answer: No, the New Zealand visa fee is non-refundable regardless of the outcome. Even if your visa is declined, you cannot get a refund. Starting {{price}} through Times of Visa, the fee covers visa processing, IVL levy, and document support.

faq.question: What is the IVL fee for New Zealand visa?
faq.answer: The International Visitor Conservation and Tourism Levy (IVL) is a mandatory charge for most visitors to New Zealand. It contributes to conservation and tourism infrastructure. The IVL is already included in {{price}} when you apply through Times of Visa.

faq.question: How can I pay New Zealand visa fee from India?
faq.answer: New Zealand visa fee can be paid online using international credit/debit cards. Ensure your card is enabled for international transactions.

faq.question: Are there additional costs apart from visa fee?
faq.answer: Yes. Additional costs include: Chest X-ray (₹1,500-3,000), visa center service fee for paper applications (₹1,715), travel insurance (recommended), certified translations if documents not in English.

faq.question: Is New Zealand visa fee increasing in 2026?
faq.answer: Visa center service fees increased from January 2026 by 10-20%. Immigration NZ fees and IVL may also change. Starting {{price}} through Times of Visa — our pricing is kept updated automatically.

faq.question: How long does New Zealand visa processing take from India?
faq.answer: New Zealand visa processing typically takes {{processing_time}} for Indian applicants. 80% of applications are decided within 3.5 weeks. Some applications may take up to 4 weeks if additional documents are requested.

faq.question: Can I get New Zealand visa urgently?
faq.answer: Immigration NZ does not offer expedited processing for visitor visas. Apply at least 6-8 weeks before travel to account for any delays. For genuine emergencies, contact Immigration NZ directly.

faq.question: When should I apply for New Zealand visa?
faq.answer: Apply 6-8 weeks before your intended travel date. This gives time for processing, any additional document requests, and arranging the mandatory chest X-ray if not done already.

faq.question: How can I track my New Zealand visa application?
faq.answer: Track your application by logging into your RealMe account on the Immigration NZ website (immigration.govt.nz). You will also receive email updates at key stages of processing.

faq.question: What if Immigration NZ requests additional documents?
faq.answer: Respond promptly to any document requests via your RealMe account. Delays in providing requested documents can lead to application decline. You typically get 10-20 working days to respond.

faq.question: What documents are required for New Zealand visa from India?
faq.answer: Required documents: Valid passport (3+ months validity), photo (35x45mm or digital), proof of funds (NZD $1,000/month), return travel proof, chest X-ray certificate (mandatory for Indians), employment proof, travel itinerary.

faq.question: Is chest X-ray mandatory for New Zealand visa from India?
faq.answer: Yes, chest X-ray is MANDATORY for all Indian citizens applying for New Zealand visa, regardless of stay duration. India is not on the low TB incidence country list. X-ray must be from an INZ-approved panel physician.

faq.question: What is the minimum bank balance for New Zealand visa?
faq.answer: You need to show NZD $1,000 per month of intended stay (approximately ₹52,000/month). If accommodation is pre-paid, the requirement reduces to NZD $400 per month. Provide 3-6 months bank statements.

faq.question: Do I need police clearance for New Zealand visa?
faq.answer: Police Clearance Certificate (PCC) is required only if your total stay in NZ will exceed 24 months. From December 2025, only PCCs from Regional Passport Offices are accepted - not from local police stations.

faq.question: What photo size is required for New Zealand visa?
faq.answer: For paper applications: 35mm x 45mm. For online applications: 900x1200 to 2250x3000 pixels, JPEG format, 500KB-3MB file size. Gray background, neutral expression, no glasses, ears visible.

faq.question: Do I need flight tickets for New Zealand visa?
faq.answer: Confirmed flight tickets are NOT required at application. You can show a tentative itinerary or proof of funds to purchase return travel. Do not book non-refundable tickets before visa approval.

faq.question: Is travel insurance required for New Zealand visa?
faq.answer: Travel insurance is NOT mandatory for New Zealand visitor visa but is highly recommended. NZ healthcare is expensive for visitors. Get coverage of at least NZD $50,000-100,000 for medical emergencies.

faq.question: Do documents need to be translated for NZ visa?
faq.answer: Yes, all documents not in English must be accompanied by certified English translations. Translations should be done by a certified translator or translation agency.

faq.question: How do I apply for New Zealand visa from India?
faq.answer: Apply through Times of Visa starting {{price}}: 1) Complete visitor visa application, 2) Upload documents, 3) Get chest X-ray from panel physician, 4) Pay visa fee, 5) Submit and wait for decision. Processing takes 2-3 weeks.

faq.question: Is interview required for New Zealand visa?
faq.answer: No, there is no mandatory interview for New Zealand visitor visa. The application is processed based on submitted documents. However, Immigration NZ may request additional information or clarification.

faq.question: Are biometrics required for New Zealand visa?
faq.answer: Biometrics may be required. If requested by Immigration NZ, you will need to visit a visa application center in India (Delhi, Mumbai, or Kolkata) for fingerprints and photo capture.

faq.question: Can I apply for NZ visa through a visa center or embassy?
faq.answer: New Zealand visa applications from India must be submitted online through immigration.govt.nz. Visa centers in India only handle passport verification and biometrics if requested - not application submission.

faq.question: Where are NZ visa application centers in India?
faq.answer: New Zealand visa centers in India are located in New Delhi (Connaught Place), Mumbai (BKC), and Kolkata (Kasba). These handle passport verification and biometrics only - applications are submitted online.

faq.question: How long is New Zealand visa valid for Indians?
faq.answer: New Zealand visitor visa for Indians is typically valid for 18 months. Frequent travelers with good travel history may receive visas valid for up to 5 years. Validity is determined by Immigration NZ.

faq.question: How long can I stay in New Zealand on visitor visa?
faq.answer: You can stay up to 6 months per visit with multiple-entry visa, or up to 9 months with single-entry visa. Maximum stay is 9 months in any 18-month period. Immigration officer determines exact duration at entry.

faq.question: Can I extend my New Zealand visa?
faq.answer: Yes, visitor visa can be extended from within New Zealand through immigration.govt.nz. Apply before current visa expires. Extensions are not guaranteed and depend on your circumstances and original visa conditions.

faq.question: Is New Zealand visa multiple entry?
faq.answer: Yes, most New Zealand visitor visas are granted with multiple entry. You can enter and exit NZ multiple times during visa validity period, subject to maximum stay limits (9 months in 18 months).

faq.question: Can I work in New Zealand on visitor visa?
faq.answer: No, you cannot work locally in New Zealand on a visitor visa. However, you ARE allowed to work remotely for an employer or client based outside New Zealand. Local employment requires a work visa.

faq.question: Can I study in New Zealand on visitor visa?
faq.answer: You can study short courses up to 3 months on a visitor visa. For longer courses or degree programs, you need to apply for a Student Visa.

faq.question: What is New Zealand visa approval rate for Indians?
faq.answer: The New Zealand visitor visa has a moderate approval rate for Indian citizens. Rejection rates are higher than for many other nationalities, so thorough documentation is essential.

faq.question: Why is NZ visa rejection rate high for Indians?
faq.answer: Common rejection reasons for Indians: insufficient funds, weak ties to home country, unclear travel purpose, incomplete documentation, health/character concerns. Strong documentation is essential.

faq.question: What are common reasons for NZ visa rejection?
faq.answer: Common rejection reasons: 1) Not a bona fide visitor (doubts about return), 2) Insufficient funds, 3) Incomplete/inaccurate documents, 4) Character/health issues, 5) Previous immigration violations.

faq.question: Can I reapply after New Zealand visa rejection?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reapply after rejection. Address the specific reason for refusal, provide stronger documentation, and show changed circumstances. There is no mandatory waiting period for visitor visa reapplication.

faq.question: Can I appeal NZ visa rejection?
faq.answer: For visitor visas, there is no formal appeal process. However, you can request reconsideration by providing new and compelling information. The reconsideration will be handled by a different officer.

faq.question: How to improve NZ visa approval chances?
faq.answer: To improve approval: Show strong bank balance (NZD $1,000+/month), provide stable employment proof, demonstrate property/family ties in India, include detailed itinerary, provide complete accurate documents, have previous travel history.

faq.question: Where to get chest X-ray for NZ visa in India?
faq.answer: Chest X-ray must be done at an Immigration NZ-approved panel physician. Find the list at immigration.govt.nz. Major cities have multiple approved centers. X-ray certificate must be less than 3 months old.

faq.question: Is medical exam required for New Zealand visa?
faq.answer: Chest X-ray is mandatory for all Indians. Full medical examination (physical exam, blood tests) is required if: staying over 12 months, or requested by Immigration NZ. Medical must be done by panel physicians.

faq.question: What is PCC requirement for NZ visa from December 2025?
faq.answer: From December 1, 2025, Immigration NZ only accepts Police Clearance Certificates (PCC) issued by Regional Passport Offices (RPO) of India. PCCs from local police stations, DCs, or Superintendent offices are no longer valid.

faq.question: Best time to visit New Zealand from India?
faq.answer: Best time: December-February (NZ summer) for beaches and hiking. March-May (autumn) for wine regions. June-September (winter) for skiing at Queenstown, Wanaka. October-November (spring) for fewer crowds. Book 3-4 months ahead for peak season.

faq.question: How much does New Zealand trip cost from India?
faq.answer: Budget per day: Budget (NZD $100-150), Mid-range (NZD $200-300), Luxury (NZD $400+). Flights from India: ₹50,000-80,000 return. 10-day trip estimate: ₹1.5-3 lakhs including flights, accommodation, activities, food.

faq.question: Do I need travel insurance for New Zealand?
faq.answer: Not mandatory but HIGHLY recommended. NZ healthcare is expensive for visitors. Recommended coverage: NZD $100,000+ for medical, repatriation, trip cancellation. Adventure activities (bungy, skydiving) may need additional coverage.

faq.question: Can I drive in New Zealand with Indian license?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian driving license is valid for up to 12 months. Carry original license and International Driving Permit (IDP). NZ drives on LEFT side (same as India). Campervan trips are popular. Book rental cars in advance for peak season.

faq.question: What is Working Holiday Visa for New Zealand?
faq.answer: Working Holiday Visa is for Indians aged 18-30, allowing 12 months stay with right to work. Limited slots (1,000/year). Different from visitor visa. Apply through immigration.govt.nz. Popular for gap year travelers.

faq.question: Is New Zealand visa easier or harder than Australia?
faq.answer: Similar difficulty. NZ: 72% approval, online only, starting {{price}} through Times of Visa, no interview. Australia: 70-75% approval, online, no interview. Both require strong financials and ties to India. NZ requires chest X-ray for Indians; Australia may request medical.

faq.question: Can I visit Australia and New Zealand on same trip?
faq.answer: Yes, but you need separate visas for each. Popular combo trip. Apply for both visas simultaneously. NZ visa does NOT allow entry to Australia and vice versa. Some travel agencies offer combined Aus-NZ tour packages.

faq.question: Is a New Zealand visa easy for Indians?
faq.answer: New Zealand visitor visa for Indians is moderately straightforward with a success rate above 80%. Processing takes 15-20 days, costing approximately ₹13,000-₹15,000. Requirements include proof of funds, return ticket, and employment/education documents.

faq.answer: New Zealand visa for Indians starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa, which includes application processing, document verification, and expert support. Book online above for current pricing and visa plans.

oman.visaDescription: Indian passport holders need a visa to visit Oman. The e-Visa is available for tourism, business, and family visits with a simple online application process.
oman.overview: Oman is an emerging gem in the Middle East, known for its stunning natural beauty, rich Arabian heritage, and warm hospitality. From the modern capital of Muscat with its grand mosques and souqs to the dramatic Hajar Mountains, vast desert dunes of Wahiba Sands, and pristine beaches of Salalah, Oman offers a diverse travel experience. It is one of the safest countries in the region and increasingly popular among Indian travellers for both weekend getaways and extended holidays.

faq.question: What is the Oman visa fee for Indians in 2026?
faq.answer: Oman visa for Indians starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa, which includes application processing, document verification, and expert support. Multiple visa durations available (10-day, 30-day, 90-day, 1-year). Book online above for current pricing and visa plans.

faq.question: Which Oman visa type should I choose?
faq.answer: 10-day visa is ideal for weekend getaways from India. 30-day visa is the most popular for full tourism trips. 90-day visa is for extended stays. 1-year multiple entry is best for frequent travellers.

faq.question: How long does Oman visa processing take?
faq.answer: Oman e-Visa processing typically takes {{processing_time}}. Many applications are approved within 24 hours.

faq.question: What documents are required for Oman visa?
faq.answer: Required documents: valid passport (6+ months validity), passport-size photograph, confirmed return flight tickets, hotel booking, and bank statement showing sufficient funds.

faq.question: Is travel insurance required for Oman visa?
faq.answer: Travel insurance is not mandatory for Oman e-Visa but is recommended. Medical treatment in Oman can be expensive for foreign nationals.

faq.question: How long can I stay in Oman with a tourist visa?
faq.answer: Stay depends on your visa type: 10 days, 30 days, or 90 days. The 1-year multiple entry visa allows stays of up to 30 days per visit.

faq.question: Can I extend my Oman visa?
faq.answer: Oman tourist e-Visa extensions are generally not available. If you need a longer stay, apply for the appropriate visa type (30-day or 90-day) from the start.

faq.question: What happens if I overstay my Oman visa?
faq.answer: Overstaying results in a fine of OMR 10 per day. Prolonged overstays can lead to deportation and future entry bans.

faq.question: Can I work in Oman on a tourist visa?
faq.answer: No, tourist visa does not permit employment. Working on a tourist visa is illegal and results in fines, deportation, and entry bans. A separate work visa is required.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Oman?
faq.answer: October to March is the best time to visit when temperatures are pleasant (20-30°C). Salalah is best during the Khareef season (June-September) when monsoon rains make it green and cool.

faq.question: Are there direct flights from India to Oman?
faq.answer: Yes, direct flights operate from Delhi, Mumbai, Kochi, Hyderabad, Lucknow, and other Indian cities to Muscat. Flight time is 3-4 hours.

faq.question: What are the must-visit places in Oman?
faq.answer: Top destinations: Muscat (Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque, Mutrah Souq), Nizwa (fort, souq, date plantations), Wahiba Sands (desert camping), Jebel Akhdar (mountain village), Wadi Shab (natural pools), Sur (dhow building), and Salalah (Khareef monsoon, beaches).

faq.question: Is Oman safe for Indian travellers?
faq.answer: Oman is one of the safest countries in the Middle East and globally. Crime rates are very low. Indian nationals form a significant part of the expatriate community, making it culturally comfortable for Indian travellers.

faq.question: Can I drive in Oman with an Indian licence?
faq.answer: Tourists can drive in Oman with a valid Indian driving licence for the duration of their visa. An International Driving Permit (IDP) is recommended but not mandatory for short stays. Driving is on the right side.

faq.question: Is Oman good for a weekend trip from India?
faq.answer: Yes, Oman is one of the best weekend destinations for Indians. Direct flights take 3-4 hours, the 10-day visa is affordable, and Muscat offers excellent experiences within 2-3 days.

faq.question: Is alcohol allowed in Oman?
faq.answer: Alcohol is available in licensed hotels, restaurants, and bars in Oman. It is illegal to consume alcohol in public places or to be visibly intoxicated in public.

faq.question: What currency is used in Oman?
faq.answer: Omani Rial (OMR) is the official currency. 1 OMR is approximately ₹217. It is one of the strongest currencies in the world. ATMs are widely available. Credit cards are accepted in most places.

faq.question: Can I use Indian credit cards in Oman?
faq.answer: Yes, international Visa and Mastercard credit cards are widely accepted in Oman at hotels, malls, restaurants, and fuel stations. Contactless payments work in most urban areas. Carry cash for souqs and smaller village shops. Rupay cards are not accepted.

faq.question: Is Oman expensive for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: Oman is moderately priced among Gulf countries. Budget travellers can manage on OMR 30-50 per day for food and transport. Hotels range from OMR 20 (budget) to OMR 100+ (luxury). Petrol is cheap, making self-drive road trips very affordable compared to taxis.

faq.question: What if my Oman visa application is rejected?
faq.answer: Oman e-Visa rejections are uncommon for complete applications. Common issues include passport validity below 6 months or mismatched information. You can reapply immediately after fixing the issue. Ensure all uploaded documents are clear and details match your passport exactly.

faq.question: How long does Oman visa processing take from India?
faq.answer: Standard processing for an Oman e-Visa is {{processing_time}} from the date of submission. Most applications are processed within 24-48 hours. Apply at least 1 week before your planned travel date to allow sufficient time.

faq.question: Can I get an Oman visa urgently from India?
faq.answer: Oman e-Visa processing is already fast ({{processing_time}}). Express processing may be available for same-day approvals. Oman visa starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa.

faq.question: How can I track my Oman visa application status?
faq.answer: Times of Visa tracks your application and provides proactive updates throughout the process. You will be notified of any status changes or additional document requests. No need to check yourself — we monitor daily on your behalf.

faq.question: What happens if my Oman visa processing is delayed?
faq.answer: Delays are rare for Oman e-Visa but can occur if documents are unclear or information is mismatched. Times of Visa monitors your application and keeps you informed. If extra documents are needed, we guide you immediately.

philippines.visaDescription: Since 2025, Indian citizens can enter the Philippines visa-free for up to 30 days. No application, no fee, no advance processing required. Just arrive with a valid passport and return ticket.
philippines.overview: The Philippines now allows Indian citizens visa-free entry for up to 30 days, effective since 2025. No visa application, no fee, and no advance processing is required. Simply arrive with a valid passport and return ticket. The Philippines is a tropical paradise with over 7,000 islands, stunning beaches, and warm hospitality. This visa-free policy makes it one of the most accessible beach destinations for Indians.

faq.question: Is Philippines visa free for Indians in 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, the Philippines is visa-free for Indian citizens since 2025. Indians can enter and stay for up to 30 days with no visa, no fee, and no advance application required.

faq.question: Is there any fee for entering Philippines?
faq.answer: No, entry to the Philippines is completely free for Indian passport holders under the visa-free policy. No visa fee, no airport tax for entry. Departure tax is usually included in flight ticket.

faq.question: How much does Philippines visa extension cost?
faq.answer: Extension fees are set by the Bureau of Immigration, typically PHP 3,000-5,000 depending on duration. Apply before your initial 30 days expire at any BI office in the Philippines.

faq.question: Do I need to pay for tourist visa if staying over 30 days?
faq.answer: For stays over 30 days, you need an extension from the Bureau of Immigration. Extension fees start around PHP 3,000. Alternatively, apply for a 9(a) tourist visa before travel for longer stays.

faq.question: Is there any travel tax for Philippines?
faq.answer: The Philippine travel tax (PHP 1,620) is usually included in your airline ticket price. No additional tax needs to be paid at the airport for tourists.

faq.question: Do I need to apply for Philippines visa in advance?
faq.answer: No, for stays up to 30 days, no advance application is needed. Just arrive at any Philippine international airport with your passport and return ticket to get visa-free entry.

faq.question: How long is Philippines immigration process?
faq.answer: Immigration at Manila (NAIA), Cebu, or Clark airports typically takes 15-30 minutes. The process is smooth for Indian passport holders with visa-free entry.

faq.question: How to extend Philippines visa-free stay?
faq.answer: Visit any Bureau of Immigration office before your 30 days expire. Bring passport, return ticket, and accommodation proof. Extension grants 29 additional days (total 59). Fees start around PHP 3,000.

faq.question: Can I apply for Philippines visa extension online?
faq.answer: Contact Times of Visa at timesofvisa.com for extension assistance and guidance on the process.

faq.question: What documents are needed for Philippines entry?
faq.answer: For visa-free entry: Valid passport (6 months), return/onward ticket, and hotel booking or host details. No bank statements, employment letter, or other documents required.

faq.question: Do I need return ticket for Philippines?
faq.answer: Yes, a confirmed return or onward travel ticket is mandatory. Airlines may not allow boarding and immigration may deny entry without it. Book before traveling.

faq.question: Is travel insurance required for Philippines?
faq.answer: Travel insurance is not mandatory but strongly recommended. Philippines healthcare can be expensive. Ensure coverage for medical emergencies, especially for island activities.

faq.question: Do I need hotel booking for Philippines?
faq.answer: Carry hotel booking confirmation or host details (name, address, contact) for the immigration arrival card. Not formally verified but good to have ready.

faq.question: What passport validity is needed for Philippines?
faq.answer: Your Indian passport must have at least 6 months validity from your date of arrival in the Philippines. Renew your passport if it has less than 6 months validity.

faq.question: How long can Indians stay in Philippines without visa?
faq.answer: Indians can stay in the Philippines for 30 days visa-free. This can be extended by 29 days at the Bureau of Immigration for a total of 59 days.

faq.question: Can I extend my Philippines stay beyond 30 days?
faq.answer: Yes, visit the Bureau of Immigration before your 30 days expire. You can extend by 29 days (total 59 days). Further extensions possible with tourist visa conversion.

faq.question: What happens if I overstay in Philippines?
faq.answer: Overstaying leads to fines (PHP 500/day initially, increasing over time), possible detention, deportation, and blacklisting. Always extend legally or exit before expiry.

faq.question: Can I work in Philippines on visa-free entry?
faq.answer: No, visa-free entry is for tourism only. Working requires a proper work visa (9g) and Alien Employment Permit. Violation leads to deportation and ban.

faq.question: Can I study in Philippines on visa-free entry?
faq.answer: No, studying requires a student visa (9f). Visa-free entry is only for tourism, family visits, and short business meetings. Apply for appropriate visa through Times of Visa at timesofvisa.com.

faq.question: Which airports accept visa-free entry in Philippines?
faq.answer: All Philippine international airports accept visa-free entry: Manila (NAIA), Cebu (Mactan), Clark, Davao, Kalibo (for Boracay), Puerto Princesa (for Palawan), and others.

faq.question: Can I enter Philippines by sea on visa-free?
faq.answer: Visa-free entry is primarily for air arrivals at international airports. For sea arrivals, check with the Bureau of Immigration as policies may vary by port.

faq.question: Do I need to fill any form for Philippines entry?
faq.answer: Yes, fill the eTravel form online (etravel.gov.ph) before arrival. This is a health and immigration declaration required for all travelers. Free to fill.

faq.question: When did Philippines become visa-free for Indians?
faq.answer: The Philippines implemented visa-free entry for Indian passport holders in 2025 as part of its tourism promotion efforts. Indians can stay up to 30 days without a visa.

faq.question: Is Philippines safe for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: The Philippines is generally safe for tourists in popular areas like Manila, Cebu, Palawan, and Boracay. Exercise caution in Mindanao (southern region). Avoid isolated areas at night.

faq.question: Best time to visit Philippines from India?
faq.answer: December to May is the best time (dry season). June to November is typhoon season with heavy rains. Peak tourist season is December-February.

faq.question: Are there direct flights from India to Philippines?
faq.answer: Direct flights operate from Delhi and Mumbai to Manila. IndiGo, Cebu Pacific, and Philippine Airlines fly these routes. Connecting flights via Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, or Bangkok also available.

faq.question: How much does a Philippines trip cost from India?
faq.answer: Philippines is affordable. Budget: ₹2,500-4,000/day, mid-range: ₹5,000-8,000/day. Return flights from ₹15,000-30,000. A 7-day trip costs ₹50,000-90,000 including flights.

faq.question: Can children enter Philippines visa-free?
faq.answer: Yes, children with their own valid Indian passport can enter visa-free. Same 30-day stay applies. Carry birth certificate for children traveling with one parent.

faq.question: Do I need any vaccination for Philippines?
faq.answer: No mandatory vaccinations for Philippines entry from India. Hepatitis A, Typhoid, and Rabies vaccines recommended. Yellow fever certificate needed only if arriving from endemic country.

faq.question: Is vegetarian food available in Philippines?
faq.answer: Vegetarian options are limited in local cuisine but available in cities and tourist areas. Indian restaurants exist in Manila and Cebu. Carry snacks for remote island visits.

faq.question: What currency is used in Philippines?
faq.answer: Philippine Peso (PHP). 1 PHP ≈ ₹1.50. Exchange at airport or authorized money changers. Credit cards accepted in cities and resorts. Carry cash for islands and rural areas.

faq.question: Can I island-hop in Philippines on visa-free entry?
faq.answer: Yes, you can freely travel between islands within the Philippines on your visa-free entry. Book domestic flights (Cebu Pacific, Philippine Airlines) or ferries between islands.

faq.question: Is Philippines cheaper than Thailand for Indians?
faq.answer: Philippines and Thailand are similarly affordable. Philippines has cheaper accommodation and activities, while Thailand has slightly better transport infrastructure. Both are budget-friendly.

faq.question: Do I need SIM card in Philippines?
faq.answer: A local SIM card is highly recommended. Globe and Smart are the main providers. Available at airport for ₹200-400 with data. 4G coverage is good in cities and popular tourist islands.

faq.question: Can I use Indian debit card in Philippines?
faq.answer: Yes, Visa/Mastercard debit and credit cards work at ATMs and merchants in cities. ATM withdrawal limit is usually PHP 10,000-20,000 per transaction. Notify your bank before travel.

faq.question: Is Philippines typhoon-prone?
faq.answer: Yes, typhoon season is June to November, with peak in August-October. Central and eastern Philippines are most affected. Palawan and western islands are less prone. Check weather before island-hopping.

faq.question: What is eTravel form for Philippines?
faq.answer: eTravel is a mandatory online form (etravel.gov.ph) for all travelers entering the Philippines. It is a health and immigration declaration. Fill it before departure - it is free and takes 5-10 minutes.

faq.question: Can solo female travelers visit Philippines safely?
faq.answer: Yes, Philippines is generally safe for solo female travelers especially in tourist areas. Filipinos are friendly and helpful. Exercise normal precautions, avoid isolated areas at night, and use registered transport.

faq.question: What is the tipping culture in Philippines?
faq.answer: Tipping is appreciated but not mandatory. 10% service charge is often included at restaurants. Small tips (PHP 20-50) for porters and guides are common. Taxi drivers - rounding up is sufficient.

faq.question: Can I scuba dive in Philippines visa-free?
faq.answer: Yes, Philippines is a world-class diving destination. No special visa needed for recreational diving. Top spots: Tubbataha Reef, Apo Reef, Moalboal, Malapascua, and Coron. PADI courses available.

portugal.visaDescription: Short-stay Schengen visa for tourism, business, or visiting friends and family.
portugal.overview: Portugal is a rising destination for Indian travellers, with a special historic connection through Goa (a Portuguese colony until 1961). From the vibrant streets of Lisbon and Porto to the stunning Algarve coastline and island paradises of Madeira and the Azores, Portugal offers incredible value compared to other Western European countries. Apply through Portugal if Lisbon or Porto is your main destination or first entry point into the Schengen area.

mandatory.details: Valid for at least 3 months beyond return date. Minimum 2 blank pages. Issued within last 10 years.
Standard Schengen form. BLOCK LETTERS, signed in 3 places.
2 recent photos (35x45mm), white background, taken within last 3 months. 80% face coverage.
Minimum €30,000 coverage for medical emergencies. Must cover ALL Schengen countries and entire trip duration.
Confirmed return flight booking to/from Portugal. Book refundable until visa approved.
Confirmed accommodation for every night in Portugal/Schengen. Can be hotels, Airbnb, or host invitation.
Explaining purpose of visit, detailed itinerary, and strong ties to India.
Detailed plan showing dates, cities, attractions. Include Lisbon landmarks, Porto visits, day trips to Sintra, etc.

financial.details: Last 6 months from savings account. MUST be STAMPED by bank (online printouts NOT accepted). Show €50-100/day balance.
Last 2-3 years ITR acknowledgment. Shows stable income source.
Last 3 months salary slips showing regular income.
Latest Form 16 from employer as proof of tax deducted.

employment.details: On company letterhead with: designation, salary, joining date, approved leave dates for Portugal trip. Signed by HR/Manager.
Separate leave sanction confirming approved dates and expected return to work.
GST registration, company registration, business bank statements, business ITR.

additional.details: All previous passports, especially with Schengen/US/UK visas. Proves travel history.
Property ownership papers showing ties to India. Highly recommended.
If visiting family/friends: Responsibility declaration from your host in Portugal, along with their ID and proof of legal residence.
If traveling as couple or visiting spouse in Portugal.
For business visa: Official invitation from Portuguese company/conference organizer.

faq.question: Is Portugal visa fee refundable if rejected?
faq.answer: No, the visa fee is non-refundable regardless of outcome. This is why proper documentation is important to avoid wasting the fee on a rejected application.

faq.question: Can I pay Portugal visa fee online?
faq.answer: Payment is made at the visa application center during your appointment. You can pay by cash, debit card, or credit card. Some premium services may have online payment options.

faq.question: Where can I apply for Portugal visa in India?
faq.answer: Portugal visa applications are handled by visa application centers across India in cities including New Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai, Kolkata, Hyderabad, Ahmedabad, Pune, and Chandigarh. You can apply at any center regardless of your residence.

faq.question: How long does Portugal visa take from India?
faq.answer: Standard processing is {{processing_time}}. During peak season (June-September), it may take up to 45 days. Apply at least 30-45 days before travel. You can apply up to 6 months in advance.

faq.question: What documents are required for Portugal tourist visa?
faq.answer: Key documents: Valid passport (3+ months validity), application form, 2 photos, travel insurance (€30,000), flight booking, hotel booking, STAMPED bank statements (6 months), ITR (2-3 years), employment letter with leave dates, cover letter, and detailed itinerary.

faq.question: What is Termo de Responsabilidade for Portugal visa?
faq.answer: Termo de Responsabilidade is a responsibility declaration required when staying with family or friends in Portugal. Your host must provide this document along with their identification and proof of legal residence in Portugal.

faq.question: Can I use online bank statements for Portugal visa?
faq.answer: No. Online/printed bank statements are NOT accepted. You must get physical statements from your bank with official bank STAMP and signature. This is one of the most common rejection reasons.

faq.question: What bank balance is required for Portugal visa?
faq.answer: Show €50-100 per day of stay. For a 10-day Portugal trip, have ₹50,000-₹1,00,000 available. Recommended total balance: ₹3,00,000+. Balance should be consistent over 6 months - avoid last-minute large deposits.

faq.question: What is Portugal visa approval rate for Indians?
faq.answer: Portugal visa approval rate for Indians is approximately 85%, which is among the stronger rates for Schengen countries. Well-prepared applications with complete documentation have excellent chances of approval.

faq.question: Why do Portugal visa rejections happen for Indians?
faq.answer: Common rejection reasons include insufficient financial proof, weak ties to India, incomplete documentation, and vague travel purpose. Ensure bank statements are STAMPED, include property documents, and provide a detailed day-by-day itinerary.

faq.question: Can I visit other European countries with Portugal visa?
faq.answer: Yes! Your Portugal Schengen visa is valid for all 29 Schengen countries including Spain, France, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, and more. You can freely travel between these countries. But UK and Ireland are NOT included.

faq.question: How long can I stay in Portugal with tourist visa?
faq.answer: Maximum 90 days in any 180-day period. This limit applies to your TOTAL stay across all Schengen countries combined. The 180 days is calculated on a rolling basis.

faq.question: Can I extend Portugal visa while in Portugal?
faq.answer: Extensions are extremely rare and only granted for exceptional circumstances (medical emergency, force majeure, etc.). Plan your trip within visa validity. Overstaying leads to fines and Schengen-wide entry ban.

faq.question: Can I combine Portugal and Spain in one trip?
faq.answer: Yes! Portugal and Spain share a border, making it easy to combine both countries. Apply to the country where you spend the most nights. Popular combo: Lisbon + Porto + Madrid + Barcelona. One Schengen visa covers both countries.

faq.question: Is Portugal visa easy for first-time applicants?
faq.answer: Portugal visa is moderately easy for first-timers with proper documentation. Focus on: detailed itinerary (Belém Tower, Sintra, Porto), strong bank balance (₹3L+), clear employment proof, and comprehensive cover letter. Most well-prepared applications are approved.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Portugal?
faq.answer: Best time: March-May and September-November (pleasant weather, fewer crowds). Summer (June-August) is peak tourist season - crowded and hot but perfect for beaches in Algarve. Winter is mild, especially in the south, and great for budget travel.

faq.question: Do Indian citizens need visa for Portugal?
faq.answer: Yes, Indian citizens require a Schengen visa to visit Portugal. See the pricing section on this page for current visa fees.

faq.question: Is Portugal expensive for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: Portugal is one of the most affordable Western European countries. Budget travellers can manage on €60-100 per day covering accommodation, food, and transport. Lisbon and Porto are cheaper than Paris or London. Try local tascas for affordable meals and use the excellent public transport.

faq.question: Are there direct flights from India to Portugal?
faq.answer: There are no direct flights from India to Portugal. You can connect via Gulf hubs (Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Doha) or European cities (London, Frankfurt, Paris). Flight time is approximately 10-13 hours with one stop. Book 2-3 months ahead for the best fares.

faq.question: Is vegetarian food available in Portugal?
faq.answer: Vegetarian food is increasingly available in Portugal, especially in Lisbon and Porto which have dedicated vegetarian restaurants. Traditional cuisine is meat/fish-heavy, but you can find cheese pastries, vegetable soups (like caldo verde without chorizo), and salads. Indian restaurants are available in major cities.

faq.question: What currency does Portugal use?
faq.answer: Portugal uses the Euro (EUR). As of 2026, 1 Euro equals approximately ₹93-95. ATMs (Multibanco) are widely available. Cards are accepted almost everywhere. Carry some cash for smaller shops. Forex cards offer better rates than airport currency exchange.

faq.question: What is the Portugal-India connection through Goa?
faq.answer: Portugal ruled Goa for over 450 years until 1961 when India reclaimed it. This historic connection means many Goans hold Portuguese passports, Portuguese architecture dots Goa, and cultural ties remain strong. This shared history makes Portugal a sentimental destination for many Indians.

faq.question: What if my Portugal visa application is rejected?
faq.answer: If your Portugal Schengen visa is rejected, you receive a refusal letter stating the reason. You can reapply immediately with no waiting period. Address the specific reason — strengthen your financial proof, add property documents, or improve your itinerary. The visa fee must be paid again for a new application.

faq.question: Is travel insurance mandatory for Portugal visa?
faq.answer: Yes, travel insurance with a minimum coverage of €30,000 is mandatory for all Schengen visa applications including Portugal. The policy must cover medical emergencies, hospitalization, and repatriation across all Schengen countries for the entire duration of your trip.

faq.question: Can I work in Portugal with a tourist visa?
faq.answer: No, a tourist Schengen visa does not permit any form of employment in Portugal. Working on a tourist visa is a violation that can lead to deportation and a Schengen-wide entry ban. You would need a separate Portuguese work permit or residence permit for employment purposes.

faq.question: How long does Portugal visa processing take from India?
faq.answer: Standard processing for a Portugal Schengen visa is {{processing_time}} from the date of submission. During peak season (June-September), it may take longer due to higher application volumes. Apply at least 30-45 days before your planned travel date to allow sufficient time.

faq.question: Can I get a Portugal visa urgently from India?
faq.answer: Expedited processing may be available at an additional cost through the visa application center. However, standard processing is the recommended route for most applicants. Plan ahead and apply 4-6 weeks before travel. Portugal visa starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa.

faq.question: When should I apply for a Portugal visa?
faq.answer: Apply 4-6 weeks before your planned travel date, or 6-8 weeks during peak summer season. You can apply up to 6 months in advance. Avoid last-minute applications as processing takes {{processing_time}} and delays are common during busy periods.

faq.question: How can I track my Portugal visa application status?
faq.answer: Times of Visa tracks your application and provides proactive updates throughout the process. You will be notified of any status changes, additional document requests, or embassy communications. No need to check yourself — we monitor daily on your behalf.

faq.question: What happens if my Portugal visa processing is delayed?
faq.answer: Delays can occur during peak season or if the embassy requests additional documents. Times of Visa monitors your application daily and keeps you informed throughout. If extra documents are needed, we guide you immediately so there is no further delay.

qatar.visaName: Qatar Visa Waiver / Hayya E-Visa for Indians
qatar.visaDescription: Indian citizens can enter Qatar visa-free for 30 days under the visa waiver program. For longer stays or specific purposes, the Hayya e-visa system is available. No advance application needed for the 30-day waiver.
qatar.overview: Qatar offers Indian citizens a visa waiver allowing free entry for 30 days - no application, no fee, just show up with a valid passport. This makes Qatar one of the easiest countries for Indians to visit. For longer stays, the Hayya e-visa platform is available. Qatar is a modern, wealthy Gulf state known for futuristic architecture, world-class shopping, desert safari, and cultural attractions.

faq.question: Is Qatar visa free for Indians in 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, Qatar offers a free visa waiver for Indian citizens allowing 30-day stay. No fee, no application, no advance processing. Just arrive at Hamad International Airport with a valid passport.

faq.question: What is Hayya e-visa fee for Qatar?
faq.answer: For stays longer than 30 days or multiple entries, check current pricing on timesofvisa.com. The 30-day visa waiver is completely free.

faq.question: Is there any hidden fee for Qatar visa waiver?
faq.answer: No, the Qatar visa waiver is completely free. There are no hidden charges, service fees, or processing fees. You simply get stamped in at immigration at no cost.

faq.question: How much does Qatar visa extension cost?
faq.answer: The initial 30-day visa waiver is completely free. For longer stays, a Hayya e-visa starts at {{price}} through Times of Visa. Extensions of the waiver are handled at Qatar immigration.

faq.question: Do I need to pay anything at Qatar airport for visa?
faq.answer: No, absolutely nothing. The visa waiver is free and processed instantly at immigration. No forms to fill, no fee to pay. Just show your passport and get the entry stamp.

faq.question: How long does Qatar visa take for Indians?
faq.answer: The visa waiver is instant at the airport - no processing time. For Hayya e-visa, processing takes 3-5 working days. Apply online before travel.

faq.question: Do I need to apply for Qatar visa in advance?
faq.answer: No advance application needed for the 30-day visa waiver. Just book your flight and hotel, and arrive at Qatar. Visa stamp is given on arrival at no cost.

faq.question: How long is Qatar immigration queue?
faq.answer: Immigration at Hamad International Airport is efficient. Typical wait is 15-30 minutes. Smart gates are available for frequent travelers. Arrive during off-peak hours for faster processing.

faq.question: When should I apply for Hayya e-visa?
faq.answer: Apply for Hayya e-visa at least 1-2 weeks before travel. Processing takes 3-5 working days. Only needed if your stay exceeds 30 days or you need multiple entries.

faq.question: What documents are needed for Qatar entry?
faq.answer: For visa waiver entry: Valid passport (6 months), return flight ticket, and hotel booking confirmation. No visa application, bank statements, or employment proof needed.

faq.question: Do I need hotel booking for Qatar visa waiver?
faq.answer: Yes, carry hotel booking confirmation (printed or digital). Immigration may ask for proof of accommodation. If staying with family/friends, carry their Qatar ID and invitation.

faq.question: Is travel insurance required for Qatar?
faq.answer: Travel insurance is not mandatory for Qatar entry but recommended. Qatar has excellent healthcare but it is expensive for non-residents. Insurance covers medical emergencies.

faq.question: Do I need return ticket for Qatar entry?
faq.answer: Yes, carry a confirmed return or onward ticket. Immigration may check that you have plans to leave Qatar within the visa waiver period.

faq.question: How much cash should I carry to Qatar?
faq.answer: No minimum cash requirement, but carry sufficient funds for your stay. Credit/debit cards widely accepted. Budget ₹8,000-15,000 per day for comfortable travel in Doha.

faq.question: How long can Indians stay in Qatar without visa?
faq.answer: Indians can stay in Qatar for 30 days under the visa waiver program. This can be extended by 30 more days at Qatar immigration, for a total of 60 days.

faq.question: Can I extend Qatar visa waiver?
faq.answer: Yes, the 30-day visa waiver can be extended by 30 days. Apply before the original 30 days expire. Check current extension pricing on timesofvisa.com.

faq.question: What happens if I overstay in Qatar?
faq.answer: Overstaying in Qatar leads to heavy fines (QAR 200/day), detention, deportation, and potential ban on re-entry. Always exit or extend before your permitted stay expires.

faq.question: Can I work in Qatar on visa waiver?
faq.answer: No, working on a visa waiver is illegal. Employment requires a proper work visa sponsored by a Qatari employer. Violation leads to fines, deportation, and ban.

faq.question: Can I re-enter Qatar after using visa waiver?
faq.answer: Yes, you can re-enter Qatar on a new visa waiver after exiting. There is no mandatory gap between visits, but frequent entries may attract immigration scrutiny.

faq.question: How to apply for Hayya e-visa?
faq.answer: Apply through Times of Visa for the Hayya e-visa starting at {{price}}. Upload passport scan and photo, processing takes 3-5 days. Only needed for stays exceeding the free 30-day waiver.

faq.question: Can I use Qatar visa waiver for transit?
faq.answer: Yes, Indians can use the visa waiver for transit stopovers in Qatar. Qatar Airways also offers free Doha city tours for transit passengers with long layovers.

faq.question: Is Qatar visa on arrival or visa-free?
faq.answer: For Indians, Qatar is effectively visa-free with a visa waiver. You do not apply for a visa - just arrive and get an entry stamp. No fee, no form, no advance application.

faq.question: Is Qatar safe for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: Yes, Qatar is extremely safe with very low crime rates. It is one of the safest countries in the world. Large Indian community in Qatar makes it very comfortable for Indian visitors.

faq.question: Can I drink alcohol in Qatar?
faq.answer: Alcohol is available only in licensed hotels and restaurants. Public intoxication is illegal. You cannot buy alcohol from stores. Duty-free purchases are allowed at the airport.

faq.question: Best time to visit Qatar from India?
faq.answer: November to March is the best time with pleasant temperatures (20-25°C). Avoid June-September when temperatures exceed 45°C. Ramadan periods have restricted daytime activities.

faq.question: What is the dress code in Qatar?
faq.answer: Dress modestly in public. Cover shoulders and knees in malls, souqs, and cultural sites. Swimwear only at pools and beaches. No specific dress code for tourists in hotels.

faq.question: Are there direct flights from India to Qatar?
faq.answer: Yes, frequent direct flights from Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kochi, Hyderabad, Bengaluru, Kolkata, and more. Qatar Airways, IndiGo, and Air India operate these routes. Flight time: 3-4 hours.

faq.question: How much does a Qatar trip cost from India?
faq.answer: Budget: ₹8,000-12,000/day. Return flights from ₹15,000-30,000. Hotels from ₹5,000-15,000/night. A 4-5 day trip costs approximately ₹60,000-1,20,000 including flights.

faq.question: Can I use Indian driving license in Qatar?
faq.answer: Indian driving license is not valid in Qatar. You need an International Driving Permit (IDP) to rent a car. Alternatively, use Uber, Karwa taxis, or Doha Metro.

faq.question: Is there a large Indian community in Qatar?
faq.answer: Yes, Indians form the largest expatriate community in Qatar (~700,000+). Indian restaurants, shops, and cultural centers are widely available. Hindi and Malayalam widely spoken.

faq.question: What currency is used in Qatar?
faq.answer: Qatari Riyal (QAR). 1 QAR ≈ ₹23. Credit/debit cards widely accepted. ATMs available everywhere. Exchange money at airport or authorized exchange houses for best rates.

faq.question: Can children enter Qatar on visa waiver?
faq.answer: Yes, children with their own valid Indian passport can enter Qatar on the visa waiver. Same conditions as adults - 30 days free entry with no advance application.

faq.question: Can I visit Qatar during Ramadan?
faq.answer: Yes, tourists can visit Qatar during Ramadan. However, eating, drinking, and smoking in public during daylight hours is prohibited. Hotels serve food to guests privately. Many attractions remain open with adjusted hours.

faq.question: Is there a minimum stay requirement in Qatar?
faq.answer: No minimum stay requirement. You can visit Qatar for a day trip, weekend, or up to 30 days on the visa waiver. Qatar Airways even offers free transit tours for passengers with long layovers.

faq.question: Can I enter Qatar multiple times on visa waiver?
faq.answer: The visa waiver is single entry per visit. When you exit, the waiver ends. You can re-enter and get a fresh 30-day waiver. No mandatory gap between visits required.

faq.question: What is the best area to stay in Doha?
faq.answer: West Bay for luxury hotels, Souq Waqif area for culture and budget, The Pearl-Qatar for upscale living, Lusail for modern attractions. Most tourists prefer Souq Waqif or West Bay.

faq.question: Do I need yellow fever vaccination for Qatar?
faq.answer: Yellow fever vaccination certificate is required only if arriving from a yellow fever endemic country. Traveling directly from India does not require this certificate.

faq.question: Can I bring medicine to Qatar?
faq.answer: Prescription medicines are allowed with a valid prescription. Some medicines legal in India are controlled in Qatar. Carry prescriptions in English and keep medicines in original packaging.

faq.question: Is public transport good in Qatar?
faq.answer: Yes, Doha Metro is modern, clean, and affordable (QAR 2-6 per trip). Karwa buses and taxis also available. Uber and Careem ride-hailing apps work well. Renting a car is easy with IDP.

saudiarabia.visaDescription: Indian passport holders need a visa to visit Saudi Arabia. Tourist e-Visa, business visa, and Umrah visa options are available with online application.
saudiarabia.overview: Saudi Arabia has transformed into a major tourism destination, welcoming millions of visitors annually. Beyond the holy cities of Mecca and Medina for Umrah and Hajj pilgrims, Saudi Arabia offers ancient ruins at Al Ula, modern entertainment in Riyadh, Red Sea beaches in Jeddah and NEOM, and stunning landscapes in Abha and Asir region. Indian nationals form one of the largest groups of visitors to Saudi Arabia, for both religious pilgrimage and leisure tourism.

faq.question: What is the Saudi Arabia visa fee for Indians in 2026?
