faq.question: Is Saudi tourist visa fee refundable?
faq.answer: No, the visa fee is non-refundable once the application is submitted for processing.

faq.question: Does Saudi tourist e-Visa include insurance?
faq.answer: Yes, the Saudi tourist e-Visa fee includes mandatory basic medical insurance covering emergencies during your stay.

faq.question: How long does Saudi Arabia visa processing take?
faq.answer: Saudi tourist e-Visa processing takes 5-10 working days. Business visa may take up to 15 working days depending on the type.

faq.question: What documents are needed for Saudi Arabia visa?
faq.answer: Required documents: valid passport (6+ months validity, 2 blank pages), passport-size photograph, travel insurance, return flight tickets, hotel booking, and bank statement showing sufficient funds.

faq.question: Is travel insurance mandatory for Saudi Arabia?
faq.answer: Yes, travel insurance is mandatory for Saudi Arabia. The tourist e-Visa includes basic medical coverage. Additional comprehensive insurance is recommended.

faq.question: How long can I stay in Saudi Arabia on tourist visa?
faq.answer: The Saudi tourist e-Visa allows a maximum stay of 90 days per visit. The visa is valid for 1 year with multiple entries.

faq.question: Is Saudi tourist visa multiple entry?
faq.answer: Yes, the Saudi tourist e-Visa is multiple entry, valid for 1 year. You can enter Saudi Arabia multiple times within the validity period, with each stay not exceeding 90 days.

faq.question: Can I perform Umrah on a tourist visa?
faq.answer: Yes, holders of Saudi tourist e-Visa can perform Umrah. However, during Hajj season, a separate Hajj visa is required. Tourist visa does not cover Hajj.

faq.question: What is the difference between Umrah visa and tourist visa?
faq.answer: Umrah visa is specifically for pilgrimage and is typically arranged through authorized Umrah operators. Tourist e-Visa allows Umrah plus general tourism. Both allow visits to Mecca and Medina (for Muslims only).

faq.question: Can non-Muslims visit Saudi Arabia?
faq.answer: Yes, non-Muslims can visit Saudi Arabia on a tourist e-Visa. However, non-Muslims are not permitted to enter the holy cities of Mecca and the sacred areas of Medina.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Saudi Arabia?
faq.answer: October to March is the best time to visit when temperatures are pleasant (20-30°C). Summers (June-August) can exceed 45°C in most regions. Avoid Hajj season unless performing Hajj.

faq.question: Are there direct flights from India to Saudi Arabia?
faq.answer: Yes, direct flights operate from Delhi, Mumbai, Hyderabad, Kochi, Lucknow, Jaipur, and other Indian cities to Riyadh, Jeddah, and Dammam. Flight time is 4-5 hours.

faq.question: What are the must-visit places in Saudi Arabia?
faq.answer: Top destinations: Mecca and Medina (for Muslims), Al Ula (ancient Nabataean ruins, UNESCO World Heritage), Riyadh (capital, entertainment district), Jeddah (Red Sea coast, Al Balad old town), NEOM and The Line (futuristic city project), and Abha (mountain scenery).

faq.question: Is alcohol allowed in Saudi Arabia?
faq.answer: No, alcohol is strictly prohibited in Saudi Arabia. Import, sale, and consumption of alcohol are illegal throughout the country. Severe penalties apply for violations.

faq.question: What should I wear in Saudi Arabia?
faq.answer: Dress modestly in public. Men should wear trousers and shirts covering shoulders. Women should cover shoulders, knees, and avoid tight-fitting clothing. An abaya is no longer mandatory for women tourists but modest dress is expected.

faq.question: Can women travel alone in Saudi Arabia?
faq.answer: Yes, women can travel independently in Saudi Arabia. Women can also drive, stay in hotels, and visit public places without a male guardian.

faq.question: Can I work in Saudi Arabia on a tourist visa?
faq.answer: No, tourist visa does not permit employment. Working on a tourist visa is illegal and can result in fines, deportation, and future entry bans. A separate work visa sponsored by a Saudi employer is required.

faq.question: Can I use Indian credit cards in Saudi Arabia?
faq.answer: Yes, international Visa and Mastercard are widely accepted across Saudi Arabia at malls, restaurants, hotels, and supermarkets. Contactless payments via Apple Pay and Google Pay are very common. ATMs are available everywhere. Rupay cards are not accepted.

faq.question: What if my Saudi Arabia visa is rejected?
faq.answer: Saudi visa rejections are uncommon for complete applications. If rejected, you can reapply after addressing the issue — typically incomplete documents or passport validity. Ensure your passport has 6+ months validity and all required documents are uploaded clearly.

faq.question: What is the Schengen visa fee for Indians in 2026?
faq.answer: Schengen visa fee is €90 for adults. Children aged 6-12 pay €45. Children under 6 are exempt. Additional service fees apply depending on the visa application center and country.

faq.question: Why did Schengen visa fee increase from €80 to €90?
faq.answer: The fee increased on June 11, 2024 based on EU-wide inflation and operating costs. Schengen Visa Code requires fee review every 3 years. The previous increase was in 2020 (€60 to €80).

faq.question: Is Schengen visa fee refundable if rejected?
faq.answer: No, the Schengen visa fee (€90) is non-refundable regardless of outcome. Application center service fees are also non-refundable. This is why proper documentation is crucial to avoid wasting money.

faq.question: Which country has lowest service charges for Schengen visa?
faq.answer: Service charges at visa application centers vary by country. Some countries like Italy and Slovakia tend to have lower service fees compared to others. Check the latest fees on the embassy or application center website for your chosen country.

faq.question: What is the total cost of Schengen visa from India?
faq.answer: Total cost is approximately ₹11,000-₹15,000 per adult including: Embassy fee €90 (~₹8,500), application center service fee, travel insurance (₹500-2,000), and photos (₹100-300). Optional premium services may be available at additional cost.

faq.question: Are there any Schengen visa fee exemptions?
faq.answer: Yes. Children under 6 are exempt. Students traveling for study/research may get reduced fees from some countries. Germany offers free visas for EU-funded researchers. Erasmus program participants are exempt.

faq.question: How can I pay Schengen visa fee?
faq.answer: Pay via cash, debit card, or credit card at the visa application center. Some centers accept online payment during appointment booking. Embassy fee is paid in local currency equivalent of Euros.

faq.question: How many countries can I visit with Schengen visa?
faq.answer: 29 countries: Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland.

faq.question: Are Bulgaria and Romania in Schengen now?
faq.answer: Yes! Bulgaria and Romania became full Schengen members from January 1, 2025 with land borders open. Air and sea borders were already open from March 31, 2024. They are now counted among 29 Schengen countries.

faq.question: Which country has highest Schengen visa approval rate for Indians?
faq.answer: Countries like Iceland, Lithuania, Finland, Germany, and Spain tend to have higher approval rates for Indian applicants. Malta has a notably lower approval rate. Consider these trends if your itinerary is flexible.

faq.question: Is UK covered under Schengen visa?
faq.answer: No, UK is NOT part of Schengen (never was, even before Brexit). You need a separate UK visa. Ireland is also not in Schengen. Schengen visa only covers the 29 member countries.

faq.question: Can I visit Switzerland with Schengen visa?
faq.answer: Yes! Switzerland is a Schengen member despite not being in the EU. Same for Iceland, Norway, and Liechtenstein. Your Schengen visa is valid in all 29 countries.

faq.question: Which Schengen countries are best for first-time visitors?
faq.answer: France, Italy, Spain, Germany, and Switzerland are most popular. For easier approval: Germany, Spain, or Finland. For budget travelers: Portugal, Greece, Czech Republic, Hungary.

faq.question: What documents are required for Schengen visa from India?
faq.answer: Key documents: Valid passport (3+ months validity), application form, 2 photos, travel insurance (€30,000), flight booking, hotel booking, bank statements (6 months, stamped), ITR, employment letter with leave approval, cover letter.

faq.question: What is minimum bank balance for Schengen visa?
faq.answer: Show €50-150 per day of stay. For a 10-day trip, ₹50,000-₹1,50,000. Recommended total balance: ₹3,00,000+. Balance should be consistent over 6 months - avoid last-minute large deposits.

faq.question: Can I use online bank statements for Schengen visa?
faq.answer: No, online/printed bank statements are NOT accepted. You must get physical statements from the bank with official stamp and signature. This is a common rejection reason.

faq.question: How many photos needed for Schengen visa?
faq.answer: 2 recent passport-size photos (35x45mm). White background, taken within last 6 months, neutral expression, 70-80% face coverage. Must be ICAO compliant. Avoid selfies or casual photos.

faq.question: Do I need hotel booking for entire trip?
faq.answer: Yes, every night must be covered with confirmed accommodation. Can be hotels, Airbnb, hostels, or invitation from host (with their documents). Book refundable until visa approved.

faq.question: How long does Schengen visa processing take?
faq.answer: Standard processing: {{processing_time}}. Peak season (May-August) may take up to 45 days. Apply at least 30-45 days before travel. You can apply up to 6 months in advance.

faq.question: When should I apply for Schengen visa?
faq.answer: Apply 4-6 weeks before travel (minimum 15 days). During summer (May-August), apply 6-8 weeks ahead. You can apply maximum 6 months before travel - good for honeymoon planning.

faq.question: Can I apply for Schengen visa without appointment?
faq.answer: No, appointment is mandatory at the visa application center or embassy. Walk-ins not accepted. Book early as slots fill quickly, especially during peak season. Check multiple cities if your city is fully booked.

faq.question: How long is Schengen visa valid?
faq.answer: First-time applicants usually get visa matching trip duration (+ 15 days grace). Frequent travelers may get 1-5 year multiple entry visas. In 2024, 63% of Indians received multiple-entry visas.

faq.question: What is 90/180 day rule for Schengen?
faq.answer: You can stay maximum 90 days in any 180-day rolling period. The 180 days is calculated backwards from each day of stay. Use the official Schengen calculator to track your days.

faq.question: Can I extend Schengen visa while in Europe?
faq.answer: Extensions are very rare and only granted for exceptional circumstances (medical emergency, force majeure). Plan your trip within visa validity. Overstaying leads to fines and future bans.

faq.question: Can I work in Europe on Schengen tourist visa?
faq.answer: No, Schengen tourist visa does NOT permit employment. You can attend business meetings but not paid work. Working illegally can lead to deportation and permanent Schengen ban.

faq.question: What happens if I overstay Schengen visa?
faq.answer: Overstaying is serious: Fines (€200-1,000+), detention, deportation, and Schengen-wide entry ban (1-5 years). Your data is stored in EES system. Future visa applications will be affected.

faq.question: Can I get multiple-entry Schengen visa on first application?
faq.answer: Yes, possible but not guaranteed. Strong profile helps: good financials, stable job, property, previous travel history. In 2024, 63% of Indian applicants received multiple-entry visas.

faq.question: What is Schengen visa approval rate for Indians?
faq.answer: The overall Schengen visa approval rate for Indians is strong, with most applications being approved. India is one of the highest-volume applicant countries globally. Approval depends on documentation quality and individual circumstances.

faq.question: What are common reasons for Schengen visa rejection?
faq.answer: Top reasons: 1) Insufficient funds/unclear financial proof, 2) Weak ties to India, 3) Vague travel purpose/itinerary, 4) Missing documents, 5) Unstamped bank statements, 6) Previous visa violations.

faq.question: Can I appeal Schengen visa rejection?
faq.answer: Yes, you can appeal within timeframe specified in rejection letter (usually 30-60 days). Appeal process varies by country. Appeals have low success rate - often better to reapply with stronger documents.

faq.question: Why is Malta Schengen visa approval so low for Indians?
faq.answer: Malta has a notably low approval rate for Indians - among the lowest in the Schengen area. Reasons: Small country, high scrutiny, concerns about overstay. Avoid applying to Malta unless you have genuine strong ties there.

faq.question: How to increase Schengen visa approval chances?
faq.answer: Key tips: Apply to high-approval countries (Iceland, Germany, Spain), complete documentation, strong bank balance (₹3L+), stamped bank statements, detailed itinerary, employment letter with leave dates, previous travel history.

faq.question: First time applying for Schengen - what are my chances?
faq.answer: First-timers have good chances with proper documentation. Focus on: detailed itinerary, strong financials, clear employment proof, cover letter explaining ties to India. Previous US/UK travel helps but not mandatory.

faq.question: How does EES affect Indian travelers to Europe?
faq.answer: First entry: Facial scan captured (fingerprints already stored from visa application). Passport stamps being phased out. May face slightly longer queues initially, but faster subsequent entries.

faq.question: Will I still get passport stamp with EES?
faq.answer: Passport stamping is being phased out. During transition (Oct 2025 - April 2026), both stamps and electronic registration may coexist. After April 2026, stamps will be abolished entirely.

faq.question: Do Indians get a Schengen visa easily?
faq.answer: Schengen visa approval for Indians depends on strong documentation, financial proof in INR, and ties to India. Visa application centers in Delhi, Mumbai, and Bangalore process applications; approval rates vary by country and individual circumstances.

faq.question: What is the minimum bank balance for a Schengen visa?
faq.answer: There is no fixed minimum bank balance requirement for Schengen visas. However, you must demonstrate sufficient funds (typically €50-150 per day) through bank statements, FDs, or salary slips. The exact amount depends on your trip duration and purpose.

faq.question: How much is the Schengen visa fee for Indians?
faq.answer: The Schengen visa fee for Indian passport holders is €90 (increased from €80 in June 2024). This fee is non-refundable and must be paid when submitting your application at the visa application center.

faq.question: Is 2 lakhs enough for a Schengen visa?
faq.answer: ₹2 lakhs may be insufficient for a Schengen visa application. Most Indian consulates require proof of ₹25,000-₹30,000 per month of stay as financial backing. Bank statements, salary slips, and sponsor letters are accepted as proof of funds.

faq.question: What documents are required for Turkey visa from India?

faq.question: Is travel insurance mandatory for Turkey visa?

faq.question: Do I need hotel booking for Turkey visa?

faq.question: What photo specifications are required for Turkey visa?

faq.question: Do I need return flight tickets for Turkey visa?

faq.question: How long can I stay in Turkey with a tourist visa?

faq.question: Is Turkey visa single entry or multiple entry for Indians?

faq.question: Can I extend my Turkey visa while in Turkey?

faq.question: Can I work in Turkey on a tourist visa?

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Turkey from India?

faq.question: Do I need to carry Turkish Lira or can I use Indian Rupees?
faq.answer: Indian Rupees are not accepted in Turkey. Carry Turkish Lira (TRY) or use international debit/credit cards. ATMs are widely available. Exchange money at the airport or authorized exchange offices in Turkey.

faq.question: Is Turkey safe for Indian travellers?
faq.answer: Turkey is generally safe for Indian tourists, especially in major cities like Istanbul, Cappadocia, Antalya, and Pamukkale. Take standard travel precautions — be aware of your surroundings, avoid isolated areas at night, and keep valuables secure.

faq.question: What are the must-visit places in Turkey for Indians?

faq.question: Can I transit through Turkey without a visa?
faq.answer: Indian passport holders need a transit visa if leaving the airport during a layover. If you stay in the international transit area and your layover is under 24 hours, a transit visa may not be required. Check with your airline.

faq.question: Can I visit Turkey during Ramadan?
faq.answer: Yes, Turkey is open to tourists during Ramadan. Most tourist areas, restaurants, and attractions operate normally. It is respectful to avoid eating or drinking in public during fasting hours in conservative areas.

faq.question: What documents are required for UK visa from India?

faq.question: What is minimum bank balance for UK visa?

faq.question: Do I need confirmed flight tickets for UK visa?

faq.question: How many bank statements for UK visa?

faq.question: Should documents be in English for UK visa?

faq.question: Is interview required for UK visa?

faq.question: What is the British-Irish Visa Scheme?
faq.answer: BIVS allows travel between UK and Ireland on a single visa. Available to Indian nationals. Apply for Irish visa with BIVS endorsement to visit both countries on one visa.

faq.question: Can I bring parents to UK for medical treatment?
faq.answer: Yes, UK visitor visa allows medical treatment. Provide hospital appointment, treatment plan, cost estimate, proof of payment/insurance, and accommodation arrangements.

faq.question: Can Indians get an UK visa easily?

faq.question: How much does a UK visa cost from India?

faq.question: What are the changes in the UK visa 2026?

faq.question: Can I get UK PR in 2 years?

faq.question: How long does a UK visa last?
faq.answer: UK visa validity depends on the visa type: tourist visas typically last 6 months, student visas match your course duration, and work visas range from 2-5 years. Fees vary by visa type and are payable in INR.

faq.question: How long does US visa processing take from India?

faq.question: Which US consulate in India has shortest wait time?

faq.question: Can I get US visa urgently or in emergency?

faq.question: When is the best time to apply for US visa from India?
faq.answer: Best time is September to February (off-peak). Avoid May-July (summer rush for students and families). Also avoid November-December holiday season. Applying 3-4 months before travel gives flexibility.

faq.question: How long after interview will I get my passport back?
faq.answer: If approved, passport is typically returned within 3-5 working days via courier or VAC pickup. If 221g (administrative processing) is issued, it may take 2-8 weeks or longer.

faq.question: How long is US visa interview?
faq.answer: The actual interview is typically 2-5 minutes. However, total time at the embassy including security, queue, and biometrics can be 2-4 hours. Arrive 30 minutes before your appointment time.

faq.question: What should I wear to US visa interview?
faq.answer: Dress formally or business casual. Men: collared shirt, trousers, formal shoes. Women: formal wear, salwar kameez, or business attire. Avoid jeans, t-shirts, and casual wear. First impressions matter.

faq.question: Can I carry phone to US embassy?
faq.answer: No, electronic devices including phones, smart watches, laptops, tablets, and earphones are NOT allowed inside US Embassy. Leave them in your car or use the locker facility outside (₹50-100).

faq.question: What items are prohibited at US Embassy?
faq.answer: Prohibited items: mobile phones, laptops, tablets, cameras, electronic devices, bags larger than 25cm, food, liquids, weapons, sharp objects. Only allowed: passport, documents in a clear folder, small wallet.

faq.question: Can I reschedule US visa interview?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reschedule twice without losing your fee. Log into ustraveldocs.com, cancel existing appointment, and book new date. After 2 reschedules, you may need to pay the fee again.

faq.question: What if I miss my US visa interview?
faq.answer: If you miss your interview without canceling, you may need to contact the embassy. Generally, you can reschedule, but multiple no-shows may cause issues. Always cancel if you cannot attend.

faq.question: What is the minimum bank balance for US visa?
faq.answer: There is no official minimum bank balance. However, showing ₹3-6 lakhs with consistent transactions significantly improves approval chances. Balance should justify your trip expenses.

faq.question: Do I need flight tickets for US visa interview?
faq.answer: No, confirmed flight tickets are NOT required and NOT recommended before visa approval. You can show a tentative itinerary or travel plan. Booking non-refundable tickets before visa is risky.

faq.question: How many months bank statement for US visa?
faq.answer: You should provide 6 months of bank statements from all your active savings accounts. Statements should be recent (within 1-2 weeks) and stamped by the bank. Avoid sudden large deposits.

faq.question: What photo size is required for US visa?
faq.answer: US visa requires 2x2 inch (51x51 mm) color photograph with white background. Photo must be taken within last 6 months. No glasses allowed. Neutral expression, both ears visible.

faq.question: Can I carry photocopies to US visa interview?
faq.answer: Carry original documents, not photocopies. The officer may ask to see originals of bank statements, ITR, employment letter, property documents. Keep photocopies as backup but show originals.

faq.question: How long is US visa valid for Indians?
faq.answer: Most Indian citizens receive a 10-year multiple entry B1/B2 visa. Maximum stay per visit is 6 months, determined by the immigration officer at the port of entry.

faq.question: What is the US visa approval rate for Indians?
faq.answer: The US B1/B2 visa approval rate for Indian citizens is approximately 75-80%. Success depends on demonstrating strong ties to India, stable employment, and sufficient funds.

faq.question: Can I stay 6 months on every US visit?
faq.answer: The 6-month stay is not guaranteed. Immigration officer at entry decides duration (usually stamped in I-94). Spending too much time in US (over 5-6 months per year) may raise concerns on future visits.

faq.question: Can I extend my US visa stay?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply for extension through USCIS Form I-539 before your authorized stay expires. Extension requests should be filed at least 45 days before expiry. Not guaranteed to be approved.

faq.question: Can I work in US on tourist visa?
faq.answer: No, B1/B2 visa does NOT permit employment. Working on tourist visa is illegal and can result in deportation, visa cancellation, and future visa bans. You need H1B or other work visa to work.

faq.question: Can I study in US on tourist visa?
faq.answer: You can take short recreational courses (like cooking, dance) but NOT enroll in degree programs or full-time study. For academic study, you need F1 student visa. Studying on B1/B2 violates visa terms.

faq.question: What are common reasons for US visa rejection?
faq.answer: Common rejection reasons: 214(b) insufficient ties to home country (most common), weak financials, unclear travel purpose, previous overstay, inconsistent information, suspected immigrant intent, misrepresentation.

faq.question: Can I get US visa after multiple rejections?
faq.answer: Yes, people get visas even after 2-3 rejections. Key is to show significant changes: better job, higher income, property purchase, marriage, previous international travel. Address the specific rejection reason.

faq.question: Can retired person get US visa?
faq.answer: Yes, retired persons can get US visa. Show pension statements, retirement benefits, savings, property ownership. Health insurance for the trip is advisable given age. Strong ties through family/property help.

faq.question: First time applying for US visa - what are my chances?
faq.answer: First-time applicants without travel history have lower approval rates (~65-70%) but many still get approved. Focus on: stable employment, good financials, clear travel purpose, genuine intent to return.

faq.question: Which US cities are hosting World Cup 2026?

faq.question: Can I visit Mexico and Canada with US visa during World Cup?

faq.question: Will World Cup tickets help my US visa application?

faq.answer: No, VOA requires a pre-approved letter plus stamping fee at the airport. E-visa through Times of Visa is recommended over VOA for a smoother experience.

faq.question: Which is cheaper - e-visa or visa on arrival?
faq.answer: E-visa starting at {{price}} is generally cheaper and more convenient than visa on arrival. VOA requires a separate invitation letter plus airport stamping fee and longer immigration queues.

faq.question: How long does Vietnam e-visa take to process?
faq.answer: Vietnam e-visa processing takes {{processing_time}}. Some applications are approved in 3 days. Apply at least 1-2 weeks before travel for safety.

faq.question: Can I get Vietnam e-visa urgently?
faq.answer: There is no official expedited processing for e-visa. Standard processing is {{processing_time}}. For urgent travel, consider visa on arrival with pre-approved letter (1-2 days).

faq.question: How to check Vietnam e-visa status?
faq.answer: Track your application status through Times of Visa at timesofvisa.com. You will be notified on approval.

faq.question: What if Vietnam e-visa is delayed?
faq.answer: If processing exceeds 5 working days, wait 1-2 more days as delays happen during peak season. There is no way to expedite. For urgent travel, apply for VOA as backup.

faq.question: What documents are needed for Vietnam e-visa?
faq.answer: Only 2 documents needed: (1) Clear scan of passport bio page, (2) Recent passport-size photo (4x6 cm, white background). No bank statements, ITR, or employment proof required.

faq.question: Do I need bank statement for Vietnam visa?
faq.answer: No, bank statements are NOT required for Vietnam e-visa. The application only requires passport scan and photo. This makes it one of the easiest visas for Indians.

faq.question: What photo size is needed for Vietnam e-visa?
faq.answer: Photo requirements: 4x6 cm, white background, front-facing, neutral expression, taken within 6 months. Upload in JPEG format, under 2MB file size.

faq.question: Do I need hotel booking for Vietnam e-visa?
faq.answer: You need to provide accommodation address in the application form, but no booking confirmation document is required to upload. Have a hotel name and address ready.

faq.question: Is travel insurance required for Vietnam?
faq.answer: Travel insurance is not mandatory for Vietnam e-visa but highly recommended. Vietnam has good but affordable healthcare. Insurance covers medical emergencies and trip disruptions.

faq.question: Do I need flight tickets before applying for Vietnam e-visa?
faq.answer: You need to provide travel dates and entry/exit ports in the application but do not need to upload flight tickets. Book refundable tickets until e-visa is approved.

faq.question: How long can I stay in Vietnam on e-visa?
faq.answer: Vietnam e-visa allows stays up to 90 days per entry. Single entry means one continuous stay. Multiple entry allows leaving and re-entering within the 90-day validity.

faq.question: Can I extend Vietnam e-visa?
faq.answer: Yes, you can extend your stay before your e-visa expires. Contact Times of Visa at timesofvisa.com for extension assistance and current pricing.

faq.question: What happens if I overstay in Vietnam?
faq.answer: Overstaying in Vietnam incurs daily fines. Prolonged overstay may lead to detention and deportation. Always extend before expiry or exit on time.

faq.question: Can I change entry port after e-visa is issued?
faq.answer: No, you cannot change the entry or exit port after the e-visa is issued. If your plans change, you must apply for a new e-visa with the correct ports.

faq.question: Can I re-enter Vietnam on the same e-visa?
faq.answer: Only if you have a multiple entry e-visa. Single entry e-visa becomes invalid once you exit Vietnam. For multiple entries, you can re-enter within the 90-day validity.

faq.question: Can I apply Vietnam e-visa from mobile phone?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply through Times of Visa on your mobile browser at timesofvisa.com. Use a stable internet connection.

faq.question: What entry ports are available for Vietnam e-visa?
faq.answer: E-visa is valid at all international airports (Hanoi, Ho Chi Minh City, Da Nang, etc.), major land borders, and seaports. Select your entry port carefully as it cannot be changed.

faq.question: Do I need to visit Vietnam embassy for e-visa?
faq.answer: No, Vietnam e-visa is 100% online. No embassy or consulate visit needed. Apply through Times of Visa at timesofvisa.com and receive your e-visa digitally.

faq.question: What if I made an error on my Vietnam e-visa?
faq.answer: If the e-visa has incorrect details (wrong name, passport number), you cannot edit it after submission. You must apply for a new e-visa with correct information. Fee is not refundable for old one.

faq.question: Can I work in Vietnam on e-visa?
faq.answer: No, the e-visa is for tourism, visiting family, or short business meetings only. Working requires a proper work permit and business visa. Violation leads to fines and deportation.

faq.question: Is Vietnam safe for Indian tourists?
faq.answer: Yes, Vietnam is very safe for Indian tourists. Low crime rate, friendly locals, and popular tourist infrastructure. Take normal precautions against petty theft in crowded areas.

faq.question: Can I travel from Vietnam to neighboring countries?
faq.answer: Yes, Vietnam shares borders with Cambodia, Laos, and China. With a multiple entry e-visa, you can exit and re-enter Vietnam. Single entry requires a new visa for re-entry.

faq.question: What if my Vietnam e-visa is rejected?
faq.answer: Rejections are rare for genuine applicants. If rejected, check the reason, fix the issue (usually photo quality or wrong info), and reapply. Fee for previous application is not refunded.

faq.question: Best time to visit Vietnam from India?
faq.answer: Best times vary by region: North Vietnam (Oct-Apr), Central Vietnam (Feb-May), South Vietnam (Dec-Apr). Avoid monsoon season (May-Nov in south, Sep-Dec in central).

faq.question: Are there direct flights from India to Vietnam?
faq.answer: Direct flights operate from Delhi and Mumbai to Hanoi and Ho Chi Minh City via VietJet and IndiGo. Flight time is approximately 4-5 hours. Many connecting options via Bangkok and Singapore.

faq.question: How much does a Vietnam trip cost from India?
faq.answer: Vietnam is very affordable. Budget: ₹2,000-3,000/day, mid-range: ₹4,000-6,000/day. Flights from ₹15,000-25,000 return. A 7-day trip costs ₹40,000-70,000 including flights.

faq.question: Can children get Vietnam e-visa?
faq.answer: Yes, children need their own passport and separate e-visa application. Same process and fee as adults. Parents apply on behalf of minor children.

faq.question: Do I need vaccination for Vietnam?
faq.answer: No mandatory vaccinations for Vietnam entry from India. Hepatitis A, Typhoid, and Tetanus recommended. Consult your doctor 4-6 weeks before travel.

faq.question: Can I get Vietnam visa on arrival without e-visa?
faq.answer: VOA requires a pre-approved invitation letter plus stamping fee at the airport. E-visa through Times of Visa is simpler and more convenient overall.

faq.question: Can I use Indian debit card in Vietnam?
faq.answer: Yes, Visa/Mastercard debit and credit cards work at ATMs and many merchants. ATMs are widely available. Withdrawal limits vary (2-5 million VND per transaction). Notify your bank before travel.

faq.question: What is the currency in Vietnam?
faq.answer: Vietnamese Dong (VND). 1 INR ≈ 300 VND. Large denominations used (100,000-500,000 VND notes). Exchange USD at banks or gold shops for best rates. Carry cash for street vendors and markets.

faq.question: Can I do Ha Long Bay cruise on e-visa?
faq.answer: Yes, any activities within Vietnam are freely allowed on the e-visa. Ha Long Bay cruises (1-3 days) are among the top experiences. Book through reputable agencies. Budget from ₹5,000 for day trip.

faq.question: Is Grab app available in Vietnam?
faq.answer: Yes, Grab is the most popular ride-hailing app in Vietnam (like Uber). Available for cars and motorbikes. Very affordable - a 10km ride costs ₹150-250. Download before arriving.
